Index: docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md
===================================================================
--- docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md	
+++ docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+name: orchestration-ui-contract
+description: UI contract for the phase 01 orchestration prototype
+author: OpenCode
+version: v0.0.1
+---
+
+# Orchestration UI Contract
+
+See also: [[Running-Agents-Prototype]]
+
+## Inbound Messages
+
+- `orchestration/ready`: webview asks the extension for the current snapshot.
+- `orchestration/refresh`: webview asks the extension to refresh cloud polling and republish the snapshot.
+- `orchestration/open-session`: webview asks the extension to open the selected session in the best existing surface.
+- `orchestration/open-existing-surface`: webview asks the extension to reveal the original surface for a session source.
+- `orchestration/open-external`: webview asks the extension to open an external provider URL.
+
+## Outbound Messages
+
+- `orchestration/snapshot`: extension publishes the normalized orchestration snapshot.
+
+## Snapshot Shape
+
+- `sessions[]`: normalized session cards with `id`, `source`, `sourceSessionId`, `title`, `agentName`, `state`, `bucket`, timestamps, blocking metadata, worktree metadata, and open-session routing hints.
+- `activeProvider`: current cloud provider metadata when present.
+- `generatedAt`: snapshot creation timestamp.
+- `degradedReasons[]`: user-facing degraded-state explanations.
+
+## Empty and Degraded States
+
+- Empty state explains that no local or cloud sessions exist yet.
+- Degraded state explains when cloud storage or provider wiring is unavailable.
+- Waiting sessions are surfaced as blocked work so they do not disappear inside the active list.

Index: docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md
===================================================================
--- docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md	
+++ docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,43 @@
+---
+name: running-agents-prototype
+description: Phase 01 orchestration prototype baseline for active and recent agent sessions
+author: OpenCode
+version: v0.0.1
+---
+
+# Running Agents Prototype
+
+See also: [[Running-Agents-Model]], [[Orchestration-UI-Contract]]
+
+## Goal
+
+Deliver the smallest viable orchestration surface by composing the existing Agent Chat and Cloud Agents runtime state into one read model and one webview.
+
+## Reused Modules
+
+- `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts` remains the tree-first summary for agent-chat sessions.
+- `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts` remains the canonical session-detail surface for local and bridged chat sessions.
+- `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts` remains the cloud tree surface.
+- `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store.ts` and `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry.ts` remain the source of truth for local session lifecycle.
+- `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage.ts`, `src/features/cloud-agents/provider-registry.ts`, and `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-polling-service.ts` remain the source of truth for cloud progress.
+- `ui/src/page-registry.tsx` and `src/utils/get-webview-content.ts` remain the shared page-registration and webview bootstrap path.
+
+## New Modules
+
+- `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` provides a normalized projection for local and cloud sessions.
+- `src/providers/orchestration-view-provider.ts` owns the orchestration webview binding and command bridge.
+- `ui/src/features/orchestration/index.tsx` renders the prototype dashboard.
+
+## Prototype Slice
+
+- One read model merges agent-chat sessions and cloud-agent sessions.
+- One webview presents four buckets: Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed.
+- Each card supports open session, refresh state, inspect lifecycle metadata, and jump back to the existing surface.
+- Existing trees stay intact and continue to update independently.
+
+## Constraints
+
+- No second orchestration state store.
+- No new routing layer.
+- No mutation path inside the read model beyond delegating to current commands.
+- Cloud sessions open externally when there is no richer in-extension detail surface yet.

"# Maestro System Context

You are **Dori**, powered by **opencode**, operating as a Maestro-managed AI coding agent.

## Conductor Profile



## About Maestro

Maestro is an Electron desktop application for managing multiple AI coding assistants simultaneously with a keyboard-first interface. For more information:

- **Website:** https://maestro.sh
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/RunMaestro/Maestro
- **Documentation:** https://github.com/RunMaestro/Maestro/blob/main/README.md

## Session Information

- **Agent Name:** Dori
- **Agent Type:** opencode
- **Working Directory:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode
- **Current Directory:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode
- **Git Branch:** 018-agent-chat-panel
- **Session ID:** 
- **History File:** 
- **Read-Only Mode:** false

## Task Recall

Your session history is stored at ``. When you need context about previously completed tasks, read this JSON file and parse the `entries` array. Each entry contains:

- `summary`: Brief description of the task
- `timestamp`: When the task was completed (Unix ms)
- `type`: `AUTO` (automated) or `USER` (interactive)
- `success`: Whether the task succeeded
- `fullResponse`: Complete AI response text (for detailed context)
- `elapsedTimeMs`: How long the task took
- `contextUsage`: Context window usage percentage at completion

To recall recent work, read the file and scan the most recent entries by timestamp. Use `summary` for quick scanning and `fullResponse` when you need detailed context about what was done.

## Auto-run Documents (aka Playbooks)

**Terminology:** A **Playbook** is a collection of Auto Run documents. When a user asks you to \"create a playbook,\" they mean \"create a set of Auto Run documents.\" The terms are synonymous. Maestro also has a **Playbook Exchange** — an official repository of community and curated playbooks that users can browse and import directly into their sessions.

When a user wants an auto-run document (or playbook), create a detailed multi-document, multi-point Markdown implementation plan in the `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs` folder. Use the format `$PREFIX-XX.md`, where `XX` is the two-digit phase number (01, 02, etc.) and `$PREFIX` is the effort name. Always zero-pad phase numbers to ensure correct lexicographic sorting. Break phases by relevant context; do not mix unrelated task results in the same document. If working within a file, group and fix all type issues in that file together. If working with an MCP, keep all related tasks in the same document. Each task must be written as `- [ ] ...` so auto-run can execute and check them off with comments on completion.

**Multi-phase efforts:** When creating 3 or more phase documents for a single effort, place them in a single flat subdirectory directly under `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs`, prefixed with today's date (e.g., `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs/YYYY-MM-DD-Feature-Name/FEATURE-NAME-01.md`). Do NOT create nested subdirectories — all phase documents for a given effort go into one folder, never `project/feature/` nesting. This allows users to add the entire folder at once and keeps related documents organized with a clear creation date.

**Context efficiency:** Each checkbox task runs in a fresh agent context. Group logically related work under a single checkbox when: (1) tasks modify the same file(s), (2) tasks follow the same pattern/approach, or (3) understanding one task is prerequisite to the next. Keep tasks separate when they're independent or when a single task would exceed reasonable scope (~500 lines of change). A good task is self-contained and can be verified in isolation.

### Auto Run Task Design

**Critical**: All checkbox tasks (`- [ ]`) must be machine-executable. Each task will be processed by an AI agent in a fresh context. Human-only tasks (manual testing, visual verification, approval steps) should NOT use checkbox syntax. If you need to include a human checklist, use plain bullet points (`-`) at the end of the document instead.

**Good tasks are:**

- **Self-contained**: All context needed is in the task description or easily discovered
- **Verifiable**: Clear success criteria (lint passes, tests pass, feature works)
- **Appropriately scoped**: 1-3 files, < 500 lines changed, < 30 min of agent work
- **Machine-executable**: Can be completed by an AI agent without human intervention

**Group into one task when:**

- Same file, same pattern (e.g., \"extract all inline callbacks to useCallback\")
- Sequential dependencies (B needs A's output)
- Shared understanding (fixing all type errors in one module)

**Split into separate tasks when:**

- Unrelated concerns (UI fix vs backend change)
- Different risk levels (safe refactor vs breaking API change)
- Independent verification needed

**Note:** The Auto Run folder may be located outside your working directory (e.g., in a parent repository when you are in a worktree). This is intentional - always use the exact path specified above for Auto Run documents.

## Critical Directive: Directory Restrictions

**You MUST only write files within your assigned working directory:**

```
/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode
```

**Exception:** The Auto Run folder (`/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs`) is explicitly allowed even if it's outside your working directory. This enables worktree sessions to share Auto Run documents with their parent repository.

This restriction ensures:

- Clean separation between concurrent agent sessions
- Predictable file organization for the user
- Prevention of accidental overwrites across projects

### Allowed Operations

- **Writing files:** Only within `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode` and its subdirectories
- **Auto Run documents:** Writing to `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs` is always permitted
- **Reading files:** Allowed anywhere if explicitly requested by the user
- **Creating directories:** Only within `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode` (and `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs`)

### Prohibited Operations

- Writing files outside of `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode` (except to `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs`)
- Creating directories outside of `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode` (except within `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs`)
- Moving or copying files to locations outside `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode` (except to `/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs`)

If a user requests an operation that would write outside your assigned directory (and it's not the Auto Run folder), explain the restriction and ask them to either:

1. Change to the appropriate session/agent for that directory
2. Explicitly confirm they want to override this safety measure

### Read-Only / Plan Mode Behavior

**Your current read-only mode status: false**

When operating in read-only or plan mode (`false` = true), you MUST provide both:

1. Any artifacts you create (documents, plans, specifications)
2. A clear, detailed summary of your plan in your response to the user

Do not assume the user will read generated files. Always explain your analysis, reasoning, and proposed approach directly in your response.

**Asking questions:** When you need input from the user before proceeding, place ALL questions in a clearly labeled section at the **end** of your response using this exact format:

---

**Questions before I proceed:**

1. [question]
2. [question]

Do NOT embed questions mid-response where they can be missed. Do NOT continue past a blocking question — stop and wait for answers. Keep questions concise and numbered so the user can respond by number.

### Code Reuse and Refactoring

**Before creating new code**, always search for existing implementations in the codebase:

- Look for existing utilities, helpers, hooks, or services that accomplish similar goals
- Check for established patterns that should be followed or extended
- Identify opportunities to refactor and consolidate duplicate code
- Prefer extending or composing existing code over creating new implementations

This prevents code duplication and maintains consistency across the project.

### Response Completeness

**Each response you send should be self-contained and complete.** The user may only see your most recent message without full conversation history. Ensure each response includes:

- A clear summary of what was accomplished or decided
- Key file paths, code snippets, or decisions relevant to the current task
- Any important context needed to understand the response

Do not assume the user remembers earlier conversation turns. When referring to previous work, briefly restate the relevant context.

## Maestro CLI

Maestro provides a command-line interface (`maestro-cli`) that you can use to interact with the running Maestro application on behalf of the user. Run it with:

```bash
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\"
```

### Settings Management

You can read and change any Maestro application setting or agent configuration directly:

```bash
# Discover all available settings with descriptions
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" settings list -v

# Read a specific setting
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" settings get <key>

# Change a setting (takes effect immediately in the app)
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" settings set <key> <value>

# Reset a setting to its default
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" settings reset <key>

# Manage per-agent configuration
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" settings agent list [agent-id]
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" settings agent get <agent-id> <key>
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" settings agent set <agent-id> <key> <value>
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" settings agent reset <agent-id> <key>
```

Settings changes take effect instantly in the running Maestro desktop app — no restart required. When a user asks you to change application settings, theme, font size, notifications, or any other configuration, use the CLI rather than telling them to do it manually.

Use `--json` for machine-readable output and `-v` / `--verbose` for descriptions of what each setting controls.

### Resource Listing

```bash
# List agents, groups, playbooks
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" list agents
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" list groups
node \"/Applications/Maestro.app/Contents/Resources/maestro-cli.js\" list playbooks
```

### Recommended Operations

Format your responses in Markdown. When referencing file paths, use backticks (ex: `path/to/file`).


---

# User Request

Start the development of phase 01, complete all tasks everything must work as requested. 
"
I’m locating the phase 01 document and the relevant code paths before making changes.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

<path>/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs/Initiation/2026-04-30-gatomia-orchestration-playbook/GATOMIA-ORCHESTRATION-01.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Phase 01: Running Agents Orchestration Prototype
2: 
3: This phase delivers the first end-to-end orchestration prototype for GatomIA by turning the existing running-agent, agent-chat, and cloud-agent surfaces into a single coherent experience that visibly tracks active sessions. It matters because it gives Product Engineers and Software Engineers a working orchestration dashboard immediately, proves the new direction with minimal refactoring risk, and creates the foundation that later phases will extend with hooks, visual flows, and autonomous task execution.
4: 
5: ## Tasks
6: 
7: - [ ] Establish the orchestration prototype baseline by reusing existing extension and webview surfaces before creating new ones:
8:   - Inspect and map the current behavior in `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts`, `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts`, `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts`, `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts`, `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts`, and `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx`
9:   - Identify the smallest viable prototype that can be built by composing existing session state, registry events, and view registration instead of introducing a second orchestration stack
10:   - Capture the target slice in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md` using Obsidian-style markdown with YAML front matter, links to `[[Running-Agents-Model]]` and `[[Orchestration-UI-Contract]]`, and explicit notes about reused modules versus new modules
11: 
12: - [ ] Implement a unified orchestration read model for active and recent agents:
13:   - Reuse existing session and provider contracts before creating new types; search for agent session models, lifecycle state helpers, and existing telemetry patterns first
14:   - Create or refactor a narrow service that merges local running-agent sessions and cloud-agent session progress into one normalized projection for the UI
15:   - Preserve current tree-view behavior while exposing the same normalized data to a webview-friendly bridge so the prototype has one source of truth
16:   - Include lifecycle metadata that is already available or derivable now: session id, source, title, agent name, state, timestamps, worktree status, last visible activity, and blocking status
17: 
18: - [ ] Build the first orchestration prototype view that visibly works inside the extension:
19:   - Reuse the existing page registry, panel, and bridge patterns before introducing new routing or state libraries
20:   - Add a dedicated orchestration view or panel that renders the normalized session projection with clear columns or cards for Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed work
21:   - Ensure the prototype allows at minimum: open session, refresh session state, inspect current status, and navigate back to the existing agent-chat or cloud-agent experience
22:   - Keep the UI intentionally simple but visually compelling so the user can see live orchestration progress as soon as the phase is complete
23: 
24: - [ ] Connect orchestration events, telemetry, and empty states so the prototype behaves like a real product surface:
25:   - Reuse existing event emitters, output channels, and telemetry helpers before adding new instrumentation paths
26:   - Make tree updates and orchestration view updates respond to session-store, registry, and cloud-provider changes without manual reloads where possible
27:   - Add actionable empty, loading, and degraded states that explain whether there are no sessions, missing providers, or failed status reads
28:   - Write implementation notes in `docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md` with YAML front matter and wiki-links to `[[Running-Agents-Prototype]]`
29: 
30: - [ ] Write test coverage for the orchestration prototype behavior:
31:   - Add or update unit tests for the normalized orchestration read model, lifecycle grouping, and any new state selectors or adapters
32:   - Add webview or provider-focused tests that verify the prototype renders meaningful empty, active, and failed states from representative session data
33:   - Prefer extending existing test suites around providers and stores before creating entirely new harnesses
34: 
35: - [ ] Run the orchestration quality gates and fix failures until the prototype is working end-to-end:
36:   - Run the relevant targeted tests for orchestration changes first
37:   - Run `npm run check`
38:   - Run the full affected test command if targeted tests expose regressions that require broader validation
39:   - Verify the extension can surface the new orchestration prototype without breaking the existing Running Agents and Cloud Agents views

(End of file - total 39 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# Phase 01: Running Agents Orchestration Prototype

This phase delivers the first end-to-end orchestration prototype for GatomIA by turning the existing running-agent, agent-chat, and cloud-agent surfaces into a single coherent experience that visibly tracks active sessions. It matters because it gives Product Engineers and Software Engineers a working orchestration dashboard immediately, proves the new direction with minimal refactoring risk, and creates the foundation that later phases will extend with hooks, visual flows, and autonomous task execution.

## Tasks

- [ ] Establish the orchestration prototype baseline by reusing existing extension and webview surfaces before creating new ones:
  - Inspect and map the current behavior in `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts`, `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts`, `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts`, `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts`, `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts`, and `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx`
  - Identify the smallest viable prototype that can be built by composing existing session state, registry events, and view registration instead of introducing a second orchestration stack
  - Capture the target slice in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md` using Obsidian-style markdown with YAML front matter, links to `[[Running-Agents-Model]]` and `[[Orchestration-UI-Contract]]`, and explicit notes about reused modules versus new modules

- [ ] Implement a unified orchestration read model for active and recent agents:
  - Reuse existing session and provider contracts before creating new types; search for agent session models, lifecycle state helpers, and existing telemetry patterns first
  - Create or refactor a narrow service that merges local running-agent sessions and cloud-agent session progress into one normalized projection for the UI
  - Preserve current tree-view behavior while exposing the same normalized data to a webview-friendly bridge so the prototype has one source of truth
  - Include lifecycle metadata that is already available or derivable now: session id, source, title, agent name, state, timestamps, worktree status, last visible activity, and blocking status

- [ ] Build the first orchestration prototype view that visibly works inside the extension:
  - Reuse the existing page registry, panel, and bridge patterns before introducing new routing or state libraries
  - Add a dedicated orchestration view or panel that renders the normalized session projection with clear columns or cards for Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed work

<path>/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * RunningAgentsTreeProvider — "Running Agents" tree view (spec 018, T044).
3:  *
4:  * Renders three groups at the root level:
5:  *
6:  *   1. **Active**             — sessions with a non-terminal lifecycle state.
7:  *   2. **Recent**              — sessions in a terminal lifecycle state.
8:  *   3. **Orphaned worktrees**  — worktree handles whose owner session has
9:  *                                been evicted by retention (§2.4 of the
10:  *                                storage contract). Populated by T075.
11:  *
12:  * Each session leaf carries a click command targeting
13:  * `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession` (wired by T038) and a context value
14:  * that enables right-click menu entries (e.g. "Clean up worktree" for
15:  * worktree-backed sessions, T048).
16:  *
17:  * The provider refreshes whenever either the session store's manifest or
18:  * the in-memory registry emits a change event — so tree updates are
19:  * driven from whichever authority mutated first.
20:  *
21:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/tasks.md §T042 / §T044
22:  */
23: 
24: import {
25: 	type Event,
26: 	EventEmitter,
27: 	ThemeIcon,
28: 	TreeItem,
29: 	TreeItemCollapsibleState,
30: 	type TreeDataProvider,
31: } from "vscode";
32: import type { AgentChatRegistry } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";
33: import type { AgentChatSessionStore } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store";
34: import type {
35: 	AgentChatSession,
36: 	OrphanedWorktreeEntry,
37: 	SessionManifest,
38: } from "../features/agent-chat/types";
39: 
40: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41: // Public shape
42: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43: 
44: export type RunningAgentsTreeItem = TreeItem & {
45: 	/** Present on session leaf nodes; absent on group and orphan nodes. */
46: 	sessionId?: string;
47: 	/** Present on orphan leaves; absent otherwise. */
48: 	orphanId?: string;
49: 	/** Present on orphan leaves so the right-click handler can build a cleanup payload. */
50: 	orphanAbsolutePath?: string;
51: 	/** Present on orphan leaves. */
52: 	orphanBranchName?: string;
53: 	/** Discriminator for getChildren dispatch. */
54: 	nodeKind:
55: 		| "new-session-leaf"
56: 		| "group-active"
57: 		| "group-recent"
58: 		| "group-orphans"
59: 		| "session"
60: 		| "orphan";
61: };
62: 
63: export interface RunningAgentsTreeProviderOptions {
64: 	readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
65: 	readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
66: 	/**
67: 	 * Event fired whenever the store's manifest changes. Injected so tests
68: 	 * can drive refreshes without reaching into `store.onDidChangeManifest`
69: 	 * private implementation details.
70: 	 */
71: 	readonly storeChangeEvent: Event<SessionManifest>;
72: }
73: 
74: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75: // Constants
76: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77: 
78: const OPEN_FOR_SESSION_COMMAND_ID = "gatomia.agentChat.openForSession";
79: const NEW_SESSION_COMMAND_ID = "gatomia.agentChat.newSession";
80: const NEW_SESSION_LABEL = "+ New agent session…";
81: 
82: const GROUP_LABELS = {
83: 	active: "Active",
84: 	recent: "Recent",
85: 	orphans: "Orphaned worktrees",
86: } as const;
87: 
88: const GROUP_CONTEXT_VALUES = {
89: 	active: "agent-chat-group-active",
90: 	recent: "agent-chat-group-recent",
91: 	orphans: "agent-chat-group-orphans",
92: } as const;
93: 
94: const SESSION_CONTEXT_VALUE = "agent-chat-session";
95: const SESSION_WORKTREE_CONTEXT_VALUE = "agent-chat-session-worktree";
96: const ORPHAN_CONTEXT_VALUE = "agent-chat-orphan-worktree";
97: 
98: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99: // Provider
100: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101: 
102: export class RunningAgentsTreeProvider
103: 	implements TreeDataProvider<RunningAgentsTreeItem>
104: {
105: 	static readonly viewId = "gatomia.views.runningAgents";
106: 
107: 	private readonly _onDidChangeTreeData = new EventEmitter<
108: 		RunningAgentsTreeItem | undefined | null | void
109: 	>();
110: 	readonly onDidChangeTreeData: Event<
111: 		RunningAgentsTreeItem | undefined | null | void
112: 	> = this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
113: 
114: 	private readonly disposables: Array<{ dispose(): void }> = [];
115: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
116: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
117: 
118: 	constructor(options: RunningAgentsTreeProviderOptions) {
119: 		this.store = options.store;
120: 		this.registry = options.registry;
121: 
122: 		this.disposables.push(
123: 			options.storeChangeEvent(() => {
124: 				this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire();
125: 			})
126: 		);
127: 		this.disposables.push(
128: 			this.registry.onDidChange(() => {
129: 				this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire();
130: 			})
131: 		);
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	dispose(): void {
135: 		for (const d of this.disposables) {
136: 			try {
137: 				d.dispose();
138: 			} catch {
139: 				// best-effort
140: 			}
141: 		}
142: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
143: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.dispose();
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	refresh(): void {
147: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire();
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	getTreeItem(element: RunningAgentsTreeItem): TreeItem {
151: 		return element;
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	getChildren(
155: 		element?: RunningAgentsTreeItem
156: 	): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
157: 		if (!element) {
158: 			return Promise.resolve([
159: 				this.newSessionLeaf(),
160: 				this.groupItem("active"),
161: 				this.groupItem("recent"),
162: 				this.groupItem("orphans"),
163: 			]);
164: 		}
165: 
166: 		switch (element.nodeKind) {
167: 			case "group-active":
168: 				return this.renderActiveLeaves();
169: 			case "group-recent":
170: 				return this.renderRecentLeaves();
171: 			case "group-orphans":
172: 				return this.renderOrphanLeaves();
173: 			default:
174: 				return Promise.resolve([]);
175: 		}
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	private newSessionLeaf(): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
179: 		const item = new TreeItem(
180: 			NEW_SESSION_LABEL,
181: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
182: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
183: 		item.nodeKind = "new-session-leaf";
184: 		item.contextValue = "agent-chat-new-session";
185: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("add");
186: 		item.command = {
187: 			command: NEW_SESSION_COMMAND_ID,
188: 			title: NEW_SESSION_LABEL,
189: 		};
190: 		item.tooltip = "Start a new agent chat session";
191: 		return item;
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
195: 	// Group + leaf factories
196: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
197: 
198: 	private groupItem(
199: 		kind: "active" | "recent" | "orphans"
200: 	): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
201: 		const item = new TreeItem(
202: 			GROUP_LABELS[kind],
203: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.Expanded
204: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
205: 		item.contextValue = GROUP_CONTEXT_VALUES[kind];
206: 		item.nodeKind = `group-${kind}` as RunningAgentsTreeItem["nodeKind"];
207: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon(groupIcon(kind));
208: 		return item;
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	private async renderActiveLeaves(): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
212: 		const sessions = await this.store.listActive();
213: 		return sessions
214: 			.slice()
215: 			.sort(sortByUpdatedAtDesc)
216: 			.map((s) => this.sessionLeaf(s));
217: 	}
218: 
219: 	private async renderRecentLeaves(): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
220: 		const sessions = await this.store.listRecent();
221: 		return sessions
222: 			.slice()
223: 			.sort(sortByUpdatedAtDesc)
224: 			.map((s) => this.sessionLeaf(s));
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	private async renderOrphanLeaves(): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
228: 		const orphans = await this.store.listOrphanedWorktrees();
229: 		return orphans
230: 			.slice()
231: 			.sort((a, b) => b.recordedAt - a.recordedAt)
232: 			.map((o) => this.orphanLeaf(o));
233: 	}
234: 
235: 	private sessionLeaf(session: AgentChatSession): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
236: 		const item = new TreeItem(
237: 			session.agentDisplayName,
238: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
239: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
240: 		item.sessionId = session.id;
241: 		item.nodeKind = "session";
242: 		item.description = describeSession(session);
243: 		item.tooltip = `${session.agentDisplayName}${
244: 			session.selectedModeId ? ` · ${session.selectedModeId}` : ""
245: 		} · ${session.executionTarget.kind} · ${session.lifecycleState}`;
246: 		item.contextValue = isWorktreeBacked(session)
247: 			? SESSION_WORKTREE_CONTEXT_VALUE
248: 			: SESSION_CONTEXT_VALUE;
249: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon(lifecycleIcon(session.lifecycleState));
250: 		item.command = {
251: 			command: OPEN_FOR_SESSION_COMMAND_ID,
252: 			title: "Open Agent Chat",
253: 			arguments: [session.id],
254: 		};
255: 		return item;
256: 	}
257: 
258: 	private orphanLeaf(entry: OrphanedWorktreeEntry): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
259: 		const item = new TreeItem(
260: 			entry.branchName,
261: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
262: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
263: 		item.orphanId = entry.sessionId;
264: 		item.orphanAbsolutePath = entry.absolutePath;
265: 		item.orphanBranchName = entry.branchName;
266: 		item.nodeKind = "orphan";
267: 		item.description = entry.absolutePath;
268: 		item.tooltip = `Orphaned worktree at ${entry.absolutePath} (branch ${entry.branchName})`;
269: 		item.contextValue = ORPHAN_CONTEXT_VALUE;
270: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("git-commit");
271: 		return item;
272: 	}
273: }
274: 
275: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
276: // Pure helpers
277: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
278: 
279: function sortByUpdatedAtDesc(a: AgentChatSession, b: AgentChatSession): number {
280: 	return b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt;
281: }
282: 
283: function isWorktreeBacked(session: AgentChatSession): boolean {
284: 	return session.executionTarget.kind === "worktree";
285: }
286: 
287: function describeSession(session: AgentChatSession): string {
288: 	const parts: string[] = [];
289: 	if (session.selectedModeId) {
290: 		parts.push(session.selectedModeId);
291: 	}
292: 	parts.push(session.executionTarget.kind);
293: 	parts.push(session.lifecycleState);
294: 	// T071 — surface the "blocked" flag for sessions waiting on the user so
295: 	// they don't get lost among Active entries.
296: 	if (session.lifecycleState === "waiting-for-input") {
297: 		parts.push("(blocked)");
298: 	}
299: 	return parts.join(" · ");
300: }
301: 
302: function groupIcon(kind: "active" | "recent" | "orphans"): string {
303: 	if (kind === "active") {
304: 		return "play-circle";
305: 	}
306: 	if (kind === "recent") {
307: 		return "history";
308: 	}
309: 	return "warning";
310: }
311: 
312: function lifecycleIcon(state: AgentChatSession["lifecycleState"]): string {
313: 	switch (state) {
314: 		case "initializing":
315: 			return "loading~spin";
316: 		case "running":
317: 			return "play";
318: 		case "waiting-for-input":
319: 			return "bell";
320: 		case "completed":
321: 			return "check";
322: 		case "failed":
323: 			return "error";
324: 		case "cancelled":
325: 			return "stop-circle";
326: 		case "ended-by-shutdown":
327: 			return "debug-disconnect";
328: 		default:
329: 			return "circle-outline";
330: 	}
331: }

(End of file - total 331 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * AgentChatViewProvider — the canonical Agent Chat sidebar webview view.
3:  *
4:  * Lives inside the shared `gatomia` activity-bar container (alongside Specs,
5:  * Hooks, Steering, etc.) and owns a single `vscode.WebviewView`. On hosts
6:  * that support it, the view is migrated individually to the auxiliary side
7:  * bar — see `agent-chat-entry-points.ts` for the migration logic.
8:  *
9:  * The view can show three states:
10:  *
11:  *   1. **Empty / idle** — no session bound. The webview composer doubles as a
12:  *      "new session" launcher: the user picks a provider, model, and agent
13:  *      file from the catalog dropdowns and submits an initial prompt. The
14:  *      view dispatches `gatomia.agentChat.startNew` and binds to the
15:  *      newly-created session as soon as the registry attaches it.
16:  *
17:  *   2. **Active session** — a session is bound. The view forwards transcript
18:  *      mutations from `AgentChatSessionStore` and lifecycle changes from the
19:  *      registry; the composer becomes a follow-up input bar.
20:  *
21:  *   3. **Restored** — after a reload the registry is empty until the user
22:  *      clicks a tree row; tree clicks dispatch `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession`
23:  *      which calls {@link AgentChatViewProvider.focusSession} so the sidebar
24:  *      view rebinds without going through the legacy editor-area panel.
25:  *
26:  * Multi-session UX: only one session is bound at a time (per the spec). The
27:  * webview header lists every session in the registry/store and dispatches
28:  * `agent-chat/control/switch-session` to swap bindings; the dropdown also
29:  * exposes a `+ New chat` action that unbinds the view back to the empty
30:  * state.
31:  *
32:  * @see ui/src/features/agent-chat/index.tsx for the matching React surface.
33:  */
34: 
35: import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
36: import {
37: 	type CancellationToken,
38: 	ConfigurationTarget,
39: 	type Disposable,
40: 	type ExtensionContext,
41: 	type WebviewView,
42: 	type WebviewViewProvider,
43: 	type WebviewViewResolveContext,
44: 	commands,
45: 	window,
46: 	workspace,
47: } from "vscode";
48: import type { PendingWriteSnapshot } from "../features/agent-chat/acp-chat-runner";
49: import type { AgentChatRegistry } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";
50: import type { AgentChatSessionStore } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store";
51: import type {
52: 	DiscoveredModels,
53: 	ModelDiscoveryService,
54: } from "../features/agent-chat/model-discovery-service";
55: import {
56: 	AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS,
57: 	logTelemetry,
58: } from "../features/agent-chat/telemetry";
59: import {
60: 	type AgentChatEvent,
61: 	type AgentChatSession,
62: 	type ChatMessage,
63: 	type ModelDescriptor,
64: 	type SessionLifecycleState,
65: 	TERMINAL_STATES,
66: 	transcriptKeyFor,
67: 	type UserChatMessage,
68: } from "../features/agent-chat/types";
69: import {
70: 	type AgentChatCatalog,
71: 	buildAgentChatCatalog,
72: 	type AgentChatCatalogSources,
73: 	type AgentChatProviderOption,
74: } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-catalog";
