1: /**
2:  * AgentChatPanel — extension-side webview wrapper for one agent chat session.
3:  *
4:  * Responsibilities (Phase 3 / US1 MVP):
5:  *   - Manage a single `vscode.WebviewPanel` instance per session (FR-008).
6:  *   - Load the `agent-chat` webview entry via the shared
7:  *     `getWebviewContent()` helper (registered as a SupportedPage in T004).
8:  *   - Handle the `agent-chat/*` protocol messages relevant to User Story 1:
9:  *     hydration (`ready` → `session/loaded`), input submission with
10:  *     `deliveryStatus` lifecycle, cancel / retry routing.
11:  *   - Forward `AgentChatSessionStore` mutations (manifest + transcript) and
12:  *     registry events to the webview as `messages/appended`,
13:  *     `messages/updated`, `session/lifecycle-changed`.
14:  *
15:  * Out of scope for US1 (deferred to later phases):
16:  *   - worktree banner + cleanup flow (T066, Phase 5)
17:  *   - mode / model / target selectors (T066, Phase 5)
18:  *   - transcript-archive paginated requests (can land later)
19:  *
20:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/contracts/agent-chat-panel-protocol.md §4
21:  */
22: 
23: import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
24: import {
25: 	type Disposable,
26: 	EventEmitter,
27: 	type ExtensionContext,
28: 	ViewColumn,
29: 	type WebviewPanel,
30: 	window,
31: } from "vscode";
32: import { getWebviewContent } from "../utils/get-webview-content";
33: import type {
34: 	AgentChatPanelLike,
35: 	AgentChatRegistry,
36: } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";
37: import type { AgentChatSessionStore } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store";
38: import {
39: 	AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS,
40: 	logTelemetry,
41: } from "../features/agent-chat/telemetry";
42: import {
43: 	type AgentChatSession,
44: 	type ChatMessage,
45: 	type SessionLifecycleState,
46: 	TERMINAL_STATES,
47: 	transcriptKeyFor,
48: 	type UserChatMessage,
49: } from "../features/agent-chat/types";
50: 
51: // ============================================================================
52: // Host abstraction
53: // ============================================================================
54: 
55: /**
56:  * Host hook for panel creation. Production wires this to
57:  * `vscode.window.createWebviewPanel`; tests inject a fake so they don't need a
58:  * real VS Code environment.
59:  */
60: export interface AgentChatPanelHost {
61: 	/**
62: 	 * Create the underlying webview panel. The factory returns a minimal
63: 	 * {@link AgentChatPanelLike} plus a `webview` handle the implementation can
64: 	 * post messages to and listen for incoming messages.
65: 	 */
66: 	createPanel(options: { session: AgentChatSession }): HostedPanel;
67: }
68: 
69: /** Minimal webview surface the panel talks to. */
70: export interface HostedWebview {
71: 	postMessage(message: unknown): Thenable<boolean>;
72: 	onDidReceiveMessage(listener: (message: unknown) => void): {
73: 		dispose: () => void;
74: 	};
75: }
76: 
77: /**
78:  * A panel plus its attached webview. Structural so tests can provide a fake
79:  * without importing `vscode.WebviewPanel`.
80:  */
81: export interface HostedPanel extends AgentChatPanelLike {
82: 	webview: HostedWebview;
83: }
84: 
85: /**
86:  * Default host implementation that uses VS Code's real `createWebviewPanel`.
87:  * Exposed so the extension entry point can wire it up in one place.
88:  */
89: export function createDefaultAgentChatPanelHost(
90: 	context: ExtensionContext
91: ): AgentChatPanelHost {
92: 	return {
93: 		createPanel: ({ session }): HostedPanel => {
94: 			const panel: WebviewPanel = window.createWebviewPanel(
95: 				AgentChatPanel.viewType,
96: 				`Agent Chat — ${session.agentDisplayName}`,
97: 				ViewColumn.Beside,
98: 				{
99: 					enableScripts: true,
100: 					retainContextWhenHidden: true,
101: 					localResourceRoots: [context.extensionUri],
102: 				}
103: 			);
104: 			panel.webview.html = getWebviewContent(
105: 				panel.webview,
106: 				context.extensionUri,
107: 				"agent-chat",
108: 				{ "session-id": session.id }
109: 			);
110: 			return wrapVscodePanel(panel);
111: 		},
112: 	};
113: }
114: 
115: /** Adapt a real `vscode.WebviewPanel` to the minimal `HostedPanel` surface. */
116: function wrapVscodePanel(panel: WebviewPanel): HostedPanel {
117: 	const webview: HostedWebview = {
118: 		postMessage: (message: unknown) => panel.webview.postMessage(message),
119: 		onDidReceiveMessage: (
120: 			listener: (message: unknown) => void
121: 		): { dispose: () => void } => {
122: 			const sub = panel.webview.onDidReceiveMessage(listener);
123: 			return { dispose: () => sub.dispose() };
124: 		},
125: 	};
126: 	return {
127: 		viewType: panel.viewType,
128: 		webview,
129: 		dispose: () => panel.dispose(),
130: 		reveal: (...args: unknown[]) => {
131: 			const col = args[0] as ViewColumn | undefined;
132: 			panel.reveal(col);
133: 		},
134: 		onDidDispose: (cb: () => void) => {
135: 			const sub = panel.onDidDispose(cb);
136: 			return { dispose: () => sub.dispose() };
137: 		},
138: 	};
139: }
140: 
141: // ============================================================================
142: // Panel options
143: // ============================================================================
144: 
145: export interface AgentChatPanelOptions {
146: 	session: AgentChatSession;
147: 	store: AgentChatSessionStore;
148: 	registry: AgentChatRegistry;
149: 	host: AgentChatPanelHost;
150: }
151: 
152: // ============================================================================
153: // Panel implementation
154: // ============================================================================
155: 
156: export class AgentChatPanel {
157: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.agentChatPanel";
158: 
159: 	private readonly session: AgentChatSession;
160: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
161: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
162: 	private readonly host: AgentChatPanelHost;
163: 
164: 	private panel: HostedPanel | undefined;
165: 	private readonly disposables: Disposable[] = [];
166: 	private opened = false;
167: 	private lastLifecycleState: SessionLifecycleState;
168: 
169: 	/**
170: 	 * Fired exactly once when this `AgentChatPanel` is disposed. Used by
171: 	 * the host wrapper exposed to the registry so the registry can clean
172: 	 * up its `panelsBySessionId` entry without coupling to the inner
173: 	 * `WebviewPanel`.
174: 	 */
175: 	private readonly _onDidDispose = new EventEmitter<void>();
176: 	readonly onDidDispose = this._onDidDispose.event;
177: 
178: 	constructor(options: AgentChatPanelOptions) {
179: 		this.session = options.session;
180: 		this.store = options.store;
181: 		this.registry = options.registry;
182: 		this.host = options.host;
183: 		this.lastLifecycleState = options.session.lifecycleState;
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
187: 	// Lifecycle
188: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
189: 
190: 	/**
191: 	 * Create (or reveal) the webview panel, wire message plumbing, and
192: 	 * subscribe to store/registry updates. Idempotent — subsequent calls
193: 	 * focus the existing panel.
194: 	 */
195: 	open(): void {
196: 		if (this.opened && this.panel) {
197: 			this.panel.reveal(ViewColumn.Beside);
198: 			return;
199: 		}
200: 		this.panel = this.host.createPanel({ session: this.session });
201: 		this.opened = true;
202: 
203: 		// NOTE: We intentionally do NOT call `this.registry.attachPanel(...)`
204: 		// here. The owning command handler (`handleStartNew` /
205: 		// `handleOpenForSession`) is the single source of truth for panel
206: 		// registration — it attaches a wrapper that exposes our
207: 		// `onDidDispose` event so the registry can clean up. Calling
208: 		// `attachPanel` again from this site would violate the registry's
209: 		// "one panel per session" invariant and cause initialization to
210: 		// fail (regression seen in spec 018: "session ... already has a
211: 		// panel" thrown right after the user submitted the new-session
212: 		// prompt).
213: 
214: 		this.disposables.push(
215: 			this.panel.webview.onDidReceiveMessage((msg) => {
216: 				this.handleWebviewMessage(msg).catch(noop);
217: 			})
218: 		);
219: 
220: 		this.disposables.push(
221: 			this.panel.onDidDispose(() => {
222: 				this.dispose();
223: 			})
224: 		);
225: 
226: 		// Forward manifest changes (lifecycle transitions, session updates).
227: 		this.disposables.push(
228: 			this.store.onDidChangeManifest(() => {
229: 				this.onManifestChanged().catch(noop);
230: 			})
231: 		);
232: 
233: 		logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.PANEL_OPENED, {
234: 			sessionId: this.session.id,
235: 			agentId: this.session.agentId,
236: 		});
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	/**
240: 	 * Forward a transcript mutation (appendMessages / updateMessages) from
241: 	 * external callers. Internal callers never go through this method — they
242: 	 * mutate the store directly and the onDidChangeManifest handler fans out
243: 	 * to the webview.
244: 	 */
245: 	async notifyMessagesAppended(messages: ChatMessage[]): Promise<void> {
246: 		if (!this.panel || messages.length === 0) {
247: 			return;
248: 		}
249: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
250: 			type: "agent-chat/messages/appended",
251: 			payload: { sessionId: this.session.id, messages },
252: 		});
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	async notifyMessagesUpdated(
256: 		updates: Array<{ id: string; patch: Partial<ChatMessage> }>
257: 	): Promise<void> {
258: 		if (!this.panel || updates.length === 0) {
259: 			return;
260: 		}
261: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
262: 			type: "agent-chat/messages/updated",
263: 			payload: { sessionId: this.session.id, updates },
264: 		});
265: 	}
266: 
267: 	dispose(): void {
268: 		if (!this.opened) {
269: 			return;
270: 		}
271: 		this.opened = false;
272: 		for (const d of this.disposables) {
273: 			try {
274: 				d.dispose();
275: 			} catch {
276: 				// best-effort
277: 			}
278: 		}
279: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
280: 		if (this.panel) {
281: 			try {
282: 				this.panel.dispose();
283: 			} catch {
284: 				// best-effort
285: 			}
286: 			this.panel = undefined;
287: 		}
288: 		try {
289: 			this._onDidDispose.fire();
290: 		} catch {
291: 			// best-effort: never let listener errors mask a disposal.
292: 		}
293: 		this._onDidDispose.dispose();
294: 	}
295: 
296: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
297: 	// Incoming protocol messages
298: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
299: 
300: 	private async handleWebviewMessage(raw: unknown): Promise<void> {
301: 		const message = raw as { type?: string; payload?: unknown };
302: 		if (!message.type) {
303: 			return;
304: 		}
305: 		switch (message.type) {
306: 			case "agent-chat/ready":
307: 				await this.sendSessionLoaded();
308: 				break;
309: 			case "agent-chat/input/submit":
310: 				await this.handleInputSubmit(
311: 					message.payload as {
312: 						sessionId: string;
313: 						content: string;
314: 						clientMessageId?: string;
315: 					}
316: 				);
317: 				break;
318: 			case "agent-chat/control/cancel":
319: 				await this.routeToRunner("cancel");
320: 				break;
321: 			case "agent-chat/control/retry":
322: 				await this.routeToRunner("retry");
323: 				break;
324: 			default:
325: 				// Unknown / deferred message types log but never crash.
326: 				break;
327: 		}
328: 	}
329: 
330: 	private async sendSessionLoaded(): Promise<void> {
331: 		if (!this.panel) {
332: 			return;
333: 		}
334: 		const current =
335: 			(await this.store.getSession(this.session.id)) ?? this.session;
336: 		const transcript = this.readTranscript(current.id);
337: 		const isReadOnly = current.source === "cloud";
338: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
339: 			type: "agent-chat/session/loaded",
340: 			payload: {
341: 				session: {
342: 					id: current.id,
343: 					source: current.source,
344: 					agentDisplayName: current.agentDisplayName,
345: 					selectedModeId: current.selectedModeId,
346: 					selectedModelId: current.selectedModelId,
347: 					executionTarget: {
348: 						kind: current.executionTarget.kind,
349: 						label: executionTargetLabel(current.executionTarget.kind),
350: 					},
351: 					lifecycleState: current.lifecycleState,
352: 					acceptsFollowUp: computeAcceptsFollowUp(current),
353: 					isReadOnly,
354: 					worktree: current.worktree
355: 						? {
356: 								path: current.worktree.absolutePath,
357: 								branch: current.worktree.branchName,
358: 								status: current.worktree.status,
359: 							}
360: 						: undefined,
361: 					cloud: current.cloud
362: 						? {
363: 								providerId: current.cloud.providerId,
364: 								providerDisplayName: current.cloud.providerId,
365: 								externalUrl: current.cloud.externalUrl,
366: 							}
367: 						: undefined,
368: 				},
369: 				messages: transcript,
370: 				availableModes: [],
371: 				availableModels: [],
372: 				availableTargets: [
373: 					{ kind: "local", label: "Local", enabled: true },
374: 					{ kind: "worktree", label: "Worktree", enabled: true },
375: 					{ kind: "cloud", label: "Cloud", enabled: true },
376: 				],
377: 				hasArchivedTranscript: false,
378: 			},
379: 		});
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	private async handleInputSubmit(payload: {
383: 		sessionId: string;
384: 		content: string;
385: 		clientMessageId?: string;
386: 	}): Promise<void> {
387: 		const current =
388: 			(await this.store.getSession(this.session.id)) ?? this.session;
389: 		const userMessageId = payload.clientMessageId ?? randomUUID();
390: 
391: 		// Optimistic render: append a pending user message immediately.
392: 		const pendingMessage: UserChatMessage = {
393: 			id: userMessageId,
394: 			sessionId: this.session.id,
395: 			timestamp: Date.now(),
396: 			sequence: 0, // store assigns canonical sequence on read; UI just needs ordering
397: 			role: "user",
398: 			content: payload.content,
399: 			isInitialPrompt: false,
400: 			deliveryStatus: "pending",
401: 		};
402: 
403: 		// For cloud (read-only) sessions, immediately reject.
404: 		if (current.source === "cloud") {
405: 			await this.emitUserMessageWithStatus(pendingMessage, {
406: 				deliveryStatus: "rejected",
407: 				rejectionReason:
408: 					"This is a read-only cloud session. Follow-up input is not supported in v1.",
409: 			});
410: 			return;
411: 		}
412: 
413: 		// Terminal sessions cannot accept more input.
414: 		if (TERMINAL_STATES.has(current.lifecycleState)) {
415: 			await this.emitUserMessageWithStatus(pendingMessage, {
416: 				deliveryStatus: "rejected",
417: 				rejectionReason: `Session is in terminal state (${current.lifecycleState}); cannot accept follow-up input.`,
418: 			});
419: 			return;
420: 		}
421: 
422: 		const runner = this.registry.getRunner(this.session.id);
423: 		if (!runner) {
424: 			await this.emitUserMessageWithStatus(pendingMessage, {
425: 				deliveryStatus: "rejected",
426: 				rejectionReason: "No active runner attached to this session.",
427: 			});
428: 			return;
429: 		}
430: 
431: 		// Emit the pending message up-front.
432: 		await this.sendMessagesAppended([pendingMessage]);
433: 
434: 		if (!runner.submit) {
435: 			await this.sendMessagesUpdated([
436: 				{
437: 					id: pendingMessage.id,
438: 					patch: {
439: 						deliveryStatus: "rejected",
440: 						rejectionReason: "This runner does not accept follow-up input.",
441: 					} as Partial<ChatMessage>,
442: 				},
443: 			]);
444: 			return;
445: 		}
446: 		try {
447: 			await runner.submit(payload.content);
448: 			await this.sendMessagesUpdated([
449: 				{
450: 					id: pendingMessage.id,
451: 					patch: { deliveryStatus: "delivered" } as Partial<ChatMessage>,
452: 				},
453: 			]);
454: 		} catch (err) {
455: 			await this.sendMessagesUpdated([
456: 				{
457: 					id: pendingMessage.id,
458: 					patch: {
459: 						deliveryStatus: "rejected",
460: 						rejectionReason: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
461: 					} as Partial<ChatMessage>,
462: 				},
463: 			]);
464: 		}
465: 	}
466: 
467: 	private async routeToRunner(method: "cancel" | "retry"): Promise<void> {
468: 		const runner = this.registry.getRunner(this.session.id);
469: 		if (!runner) {
470: 			return;
471: 		}
472: 		try {
473: 			if (method === "cancel") {
474: 				await runner.cancel();
475: 			} else if (runner.retry) {
476: 				await runner.retry();
477: 			}
478: 		} catch (err) {
479: 			logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.ERROR, {
480: 				sessionId: this.session.id,
481: 				stage: method,
482: 				error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
483: 			});
484: 		}
485: 	}
486: 
487: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
488: 	// Outgoing protocol messages
489: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
490: 
491: 	private async emitUserMessageWithStatus(
492: 		base: UserChatMessage,
493: 		patch: Partial<UserChatMessage>
494: 	): Promise<void> {
495: 		await this.sendMessagesAppended([base]);
496: 		await this.sendMessagesUpdated([{ id: base.id, patch }]);
497: 	}
498: 
499: 	private async sendMessagesAppended(messages: ChatMessage[]): Promise<void> {
500: 		if (!this.panel) {
501: 			return;
502: 		}
503: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
504: 			type: "agent-chat/messages/appended",
505: 			payload: { sessionId: this.session.id, messages },
506: 		});
507: 	}
508: 
509: 	private async sendMessagesUpdated(
510: 		updates: Array<{ id: string; patch: Partial<ChatMessage> }>
511: 	): Promise<void> {
512: 		if (!this.panel) {
513: 			return;
514: 		}
515: 		await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
516: 			type: "agent-chat/messages/updated",
517: 			payload: { sessionId: this.session.id, updates },
518: 		});
519: 	}
520: 
521: 	private async onManifestChanged(): Promise<void> {
522: 		if (!this.panel) {
523: 			return;
524: 		}
525: 		const current = await this.store.getSession(this.session.id);
526: 		if (!current) {
527: 			return;
528: 		}
529: 		if (current.lifecycleState !== this.lastLifecycleState) {
530: 			const from = this.lastLifecycleState;
531: 			this.lastLifecycleState = current.lifecycleState;
532: 			await this.panel.webview.postMessage({
533: 				type: "agent-chat/session/lifecycle-changed",
534: 				payload: {
535: 					sessionId: this.session.id,
536: 					from,
537: 					to: current.lifecycleState,
538: 					at: Date.now(),
539: 				},
540: 			});
541: 		}
542: 		// Transcript changes are surfaced via messages/appended when the runner
543: 		// calls store.appendMessages. The panel re-reads the transcript from
544: 		// memento and diffs in a minimal way.
545: 		await this.flushTranscriptDeltas();
546: 	}
547: 
548: 	private readonly knownMessageIds = new Set<string>();
549: 
550: 	private async flushTranscriptDeltas(): Promise<void> {
551: 		if (!this.panel) {
552: 			return;
553: 		}
554: 		const transcript = this.readTranscript(this.session.id);
555: 		const fresh: ChatMessage[] = [];
556: 		for (const msg of transcript) {
557: 			if (!this.knownMessageIds.has(msg.id)) {
558: 				this.knownMessageIds.add(msg.id);
559: 				fresh.push(msg);
560: 			}
561: 		}
562: 		if (fresh.length > 0) {
563: 			await this.sendMessagesAppended(fresh);
564: 		}
565: 	}
566: 
567: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
568: 	// Helpers
569: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
570: 
571: 	private readTranscript(sessionId: string): ChatMessage[] {
572: 		// Reach into the store's backing memento keys. The store owns its
573: 		// schema; this helper is a tightly-scoped read path that avoids
574: 		// introducing a public API just for this case.
575: 		const key = transcriptKeyFor(sessionId);
576: 		const mem = (
577: 			this.store as unknown as {
578: 				workspaceState: {
579: 					get<T>(k: string): T | undefined;
580: 				};
581: 			}
582: 		).workspaceState;
583: 		const raw = mem.get<{ messages: ChatMessage[] }>(key);
584: 		return raw?.messages ?? [];
585: 	}
586: }
587: 
588: // ============================================================================
589: // Small helpers
590: // ============================================================================
591: 
592: function executionTargetLabel(kind: "local" | "worktree" | "cloud"): string {
593: 	switch (kind) {
594: 		case "local":
595: 			return "Local";
596: 		case "worktree":
597: 			return "Worktree";
598: 		case "cloud":
599: 			return "Cloud";
600: 		default:
601: 			return "Local";
602: 	}
603: }
604: 
605: function noop(): void {
606: 	// Intentionally empty — used as a silent .catch handler.
607: }
608: 
609: function computeAcceptsFollowUp(session: AgentChatSession): boolean {
610: 	if (session.source === "cloud") {
611: 		return false;
612: 	}
613: 	if (session.capabilities.source === "none") {
614: 		return true;
615: 	}
616: 	return session.capabilities.acceptsFollowUp;
617: }

(End of file - total 617 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
/**
 * AgentChatPanel — extension-side webview wrapper for one agent chat session.
 *
 * Responsibilities (Phase 3 / US1 MVP):
 *   - Manage a single `vscode.WebviewPanel` instance per session (FR-008).
 *   - Load the `agent-chat` webview entry via the shared
 *     `getWebviewContent()` helper (registered as a SupportedPage in T004).
 *   - Handle the `agent-chat/*` protocol messages relevant to User Story 1:
 *     hydration (`ready` → `session/loaded`), input submission with
 *     `deliveryStatus` lifecycle, cancel / retry routing.
 *   - Forward `AgentChatSessionStore` mutations (manifest + transcript) and
 *     registry events to the webview as `messages/appended`,
 *     `messages/updated`, `session/lifecycle-changed`.
 *
 * Out of scope for US1 (deferred to later phases):
 *   - worktree banner + cleanup flow (T066, Phase 5)
 *   - mode / model / target selectors (T066, Phase 5)
 *   - transcript-archive paginated requests (can land later)
 *
 * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/contracts/agent-chat-panel-protocol.md §4

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * Cloud Agent Progress Panel
3:  *
4:  * Provider-agnostic webview panel for rich session progress UI.
5:  * Replaces the Devin-specific progress panel.
6:  *
7:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/plan.md
8:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
9:  */
10: 
11: import {
12: 	type Disposable,
13: 	type ExtensionContext,
14: 	ViewColumn,
15: 	type WebviewPanel,
16: 	window,
17: } from "vscode";
18: import type { AgentSessionStorage } from "../features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage";
19: import { logInfo } from "../features/cloud-agents/logging";
20: import type { ProviderRegistry } from "../features/cloud-agents/provider-registry";
21: import type { AgentSession } from "../features/cloud-agents/types";
22: import { getWebviewContent } from "../utils/get-webview-content";
23: 
24: // ============================================================================
25: // CloudAgentProgressPanel
26: // ============================================================================
27: 
28: /**
29:  * Webview panel for Cloud Agent session progress display.
30:  * Renders sessions via the shared webview React app and
31:  * communicates through the VS Code postMessage bridge.
32:  *
33:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
34:  */
35: export class CloudAgentProgressPanel {
36: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.cloudAgentProgress";
37: 	private panel: WebviewPanel | undefined;
38: 	private disposables: Disposable[] = [];
39: 	private readonly context: ExtensionContext;
40: 	private readonly registry: ProviderRegistry;
41: 	private readonly sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage;
42: 
43: 	constructor(
44: 		context: ExtensionContext,
45: 		registry: ProviderRegistry,
46: 		sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage
47: 	) {
48: 		this.context = context;
49: 		this.registry = registry;
50: 		this.sessionStorage = sessionStorage;
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	/**
54: 	 * Show or create the webview panel.
55: 	 */
56: 	show(): void {
57: 		if (this.panel) {
58: 			this.panel.reveal(ViewColumn.Beside);
59: 			this.sendSessionData();
60: 			return;
61: 		}
62: 
63: 		this.panel = window.createWebviewPanel(
64: 			CloudAgentProgressPanel.viewType,
65: 			"Cloud Agent Progress",
66: 			ViewColumn.Beside,
67: 			{
68: 				enableScripts: true,
69: 				retainContextWhenHidden: true,
70: 				localResourceRoots: [this.context.extensionUri],
71: 			}
72: 		);
73: 
74: 		this.panel.webview.html = getWebviewContent(
75: 			this.panel.webview,
76: 			this.context.extensionUri,
77: 			"cloud-agent-progress"
78: 		);
79: 
80: 		this.panel.webview.onDidReceiveMessage(
81: 			(message: { type: string; payload?: Record<string, unknown> }) => {
82: 				this.handleMessage(message);
83: 			},
84: 			undefined,
85: 			this.disposables
86: 		);
87: 
88: 		this.panel.onDidDispose(
89: 			() => {
90: 				this.panel = undefined;
91: 				for (const d of this.disposables) {
92: 					d.dispose();
93: 				}
94: 				this.disposables = [];
95: 			},
96: 			undefined,
97: 			this.disposables
98: 		);
99: 
100: 		this.sendSessionData();
101: 		logInfo("Cloud Agent progress panel opened");
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	/**
105: 	 * Push fresh session data to the webview.
106: 	 */
107: 	async sendSessionData(): Promise<void> {
108: 		if (!this.panel) {
109: 			return;
110: 		}
111: 
112: 		const active = this.registry.getActive();
113: 		const sessions = await this.sessionStorage.getAll();
114: 
115: 		const sessionViews = sessions.map((s: AgentSession) => ({
116: 			localId: s.localId,
117: 			providerId: s.providerId,
118: 			status: s.status,
119: 			displayStatus: active ? active.getStatusDisplay(s) : s.status,
120: 			branch: s.branch,
121: 			specPath: s.specPath,
122: 			externalUrl: s.externalUrl,
123: 			createdAt: s.createdAt,
124: 			updatedAt: s.updatedAt,
125: 			isReadOnly: s.isReadOnly,

**Considering session storage and event support**

I'm thinking I might need to connect session storage and the provider registry to handle changes effectively. I should investigate the various features and types of cloud agents related to event support. Searching for something like onDidChange might not be the best route, so I wonder if I can make use of polling callbacks instead. It feels like there’s a lot to inspect here to get everything functioning smoothly!

I’ve confirmed the current webview registration points and test coverage. Now I’m reading the underlying session models and cloud-agent store so I can define one normalized projection and fit it into the existing UI protocol cleanly.

1: /**
2:  * Cloud Agent Store

4:  * Provider-agnostic state store for the Cloud Agents webview.

77: type Listener = () => void;
78: 
79: /**
80:  * Creates a simple state store for the Cloud Agents webview.
81:  * Includes subscribe/notify pattern for React integration.
82:  */
83: export function createCloudAgentStore() {
84: 	let state: CloudAgentState = {
85: 		sessions: [],
86: 		activeProvider: null,
87: 		isLoading: false,
88: 		error: null,
89: 	};
90: 
91: 	const listeners = new Set<Listener>();
92: 
93: 	function notify(): void {
94: 		for (const listener of listeners) {
95: 			listener();
96: 		}
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	return {
100: 		getState(): CloudAgentState {
101: 			return state;
102: 		},
103: 
104: 		subscribe(listener: Listener): () => void {
105: 			listeners.add(listener);
106: 			return () => {
107: 				listeners.delete(listener);
108: 			};
109: 		},
110: 
111: 		setSessions(sessions: AgentSessionView[]): void {
112: 			state = { ...state, sessions };
113: 			notify();
114: 		},
115: 
116: 		setActiveProvider(provider: ActiveProviderInfo | null): void {
117: 			state = { ...state, activeProvider: provider };
118: 			notify();
119: 		},
120: 
121: 		setLoading(isLoading: boolean): void {
122: 			state = { ...state, isLoading };
123: 			notify();
124: 		},

2:  * Agent Chat Panel shared types.
3:  *
4:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/data-model.md (source of truth)

12:  */
13: 
14: // ============================================================================
15: // Session lifecycle
16: // ============================================================================
17: 
18: export type SessionLifecycleState =
19: 	| "initializing"
20: 	| "running"
21: 	| "waiting-for-input"
22: 	| "completed"
23: 	| "failed"
24: 	| "cancelled"
25: 	| "ended-by-shutdown";
26: 
27: /**
28:  * Lifecycle states that are absorbing — once entered, no further transitions
29:  * are permitted. A new run of "the same" task is a new session with a new id.
30:  *
31:  * @see data-model.md §2
32:  */
33: export const TERMINAL_STATES: ReadonlySet<SessionLifecycleState> =
34: 	new Set<SessionLifecycleState>([
35: 		"completed",
36: 		"failed",
37: 		"cancelled",
38: 		"ended-by-shutdown",
39: 	]);
40: 
41: export function isTerminalState(state: SessionLifecycleState): boolean {
42: 	return TERMINAL_STATES.has(state);
43: }
44: 
45: // ============================================================================
46: // Capabilities (hybrid discovery)
47: // ============================================================================
48: 
49: export interface ModeDescriptor {
50: 	/** Machine id (e.g. "code", "ask", "plan"). */
51: 	id: string;
52: 	/** UI label (e.g. "Code"). */
53: 	displayName: string;
54: 	/**
55: 	 * Prefix prepended to each turn's first message to steer the agent.
56: 	 * Research R1: used when the agent does not support a native mode switch.
57: 	 */
58: 	promptPrefix?: string;
59: }
60: 
61: export interface ModelDescriptor {
62: 	id: string;
63: 	displayName: string;
64: 	/** How the model is communicated to the agent at session start. */
65: 	invocation: "initial-prompt" | "cli-flag";
66: 	/** Optional template (e.g. "--model {id}" for cli-flag). */
67: 	invocationTemplate?: string;
68: }
69: 
70: /**
71:  * Reasoning / thinking effort level surfaced by the agent.
72:  *
73:  * Distinct from {@link ModeDescriptor} (which is the high-level
74:  * conversational mode like "Agent / Plan / Ask") and from
75:  * {@link ModelDescriptor} (which selects the underlying LLM). Examples:
76:  *
77:  *   - GPT-5 / Codex: `low`, `medium`, `high`
78:  *   - Claude Extended Thinking: `off`, `on` (or budget tiers)
79:  *   - Qwen Thinking: `disabled`, `enabled`
80:  *
81:  * Optional per provider; agents that do not expose a knob omit the
82:  * `thinkingLevels` array entirely so the chip is hidden.
83:  */
84: export interface ThinkingLevelDescriptor {
85: 	id: string;
86: 	displayName: string;
87: 	description?: string;
88: }
89: 
90: /**
91:  * High-level conversational role / agent type the user can pick before
92:  * a turn (e.g. Cursor's "Agent / Ask / Edit", Windsurf's "Cascade Auto
93:  * vs Cascade Plan"). Persisted alongside the session selection so each
94:  * turn can carry the user's intent.
95:  */
96: export interface AgentRoleDescriptor {
97: 	id: string;
98: 	displayName: string;
99: 	description?: string;
100: }
101: 
102: /**
103:  * Result of the hybrid capability discovery resolver.
104:  *
105:  * Discriminated by `source`:
106:  *   - "agent"   — agent reported capabilities via ACP `initialize` (authoritative).
107:  *   - "catalog" — agent silent; gatomia-maintained catalog supplied the values.
108:  *   - "none"    — neither source has values; selectors MUST be hidden.
109:  *
110:  * `thinkingLevels` and `agentRoles` are optional even on `"agent"` /
111:  * `"catalog"` results because not every provider exposes them — the
112:  * picker MUST hide the corresponding chip when the array is undefined
113:  * or empty.
114:  *
115:  * @see contracts/agent-capabilities-contract.md
116:  */
117: export type ResolvedCapabilities =
118: 	| {
119: 			source: "agent";
120: 			modes: ModeDescriptor[];
121: 			models: ModelDescriptor[];
122: 			thinkingLevels?: ThinkingLevelDescriptor[];
123: 			agentRoles?: AgentRoleDescriptor[];
124: 			acceptsFollowUp: boolean;
125: 	  }
126: 	| {
127: 			source: "catalog";
128: 			modes: ModeDescriptor[];
129: 			models: ModelDescriptor[];
130: 			thinkingLevels?: ThinkingLevelDescriptor[];
131: 			agentRoles?: AgentRoleDescriptor[];
132: 			acceptsFollowUp: boolean;
133: 	  }
134: 	| { source: "none" };
135: 
136: // ============================================================================
137: // Execution target
138: // ============================================================================
139: 
140: export type ExecutionTarget =
141: 	| { kind: "local" }
142: 	| { kind: "worktree"; worktreeId: string }
143: 	| { kind: "cloud"; providerId: string; cloudSessionId: string };
144: 
145: // ============================================================================
146: // Worktree handle
147: // ============================================================================
148: 
149: export type WorktreeStatus = "created" | "in-use" | "abandoned" | "cleaned";
150: 
151: export interface WorktreeHandle {
152: 	/**
153: 	 * UUIDv4. Not necessarily equal to `sessionId`; v1 is 1:1 but the handle
154: 	 * exists as a separate entity so future specs can reuse worktrees across
155: 	 * sessions without a schema migration.
156: 	 */
157: 	id: string;
158: 	/** Absolute path, e.g. `<repoRoot>/.gatomia/worktrees/<session-id>/`. */
159: 	absolutePath: string;
160: 	/** Branch name, e.g. `gatomia/agent-chat/<session-id>`. */
161: 	branchName: string;
162: 	/** SHA of the commit the branch was created from. */
163: 	baseCommitSha: string;
164: 	/** Lifecycle of the worktree itself (independent of the session). */
165: 	status: WorktreeStatus;
166: 	createdAt: number;
167: 	cleanedAt?: number;
168: }
169: 
170: // ============================================================================
171: // Cloud linkage (spec 016 bridge)
172: // ============================================================================
173: 
174: export interface CloudLinkage {
175: 	/** Provider id from spec 016 registry (e.g. "devin", "github-copilot-coding-agent"). */
176: 	providerId: string;
177: 	/** spec 016 `AgentSession.localId`. */
178: 	cloudSessionLocalId: string;
179: 	/** Provider-external URL (Devin session URL, GitHub issue URL). */

181: }
182: 
183: // ============================================================================
184: // Session trigger

186: 
187: export type SessionTrigger =
188: 	| { kind: "user" }
189: 	| { kind: "hook"; hookId: string; executionId: string }
190: 	| { kind: "command"; commandId: string }
191: 	| { kind: "spec-task"; specId: string; taskId: string }

208: 		modeId?: string;

212: }
213:

215:  * Delivery lifecycle for user follow-up messages. Initial-prompt messages are
216:  * always "delivered" at creation time.
217:  *
218:  * Transitions (follow-ups):

228:  */
229: export type UserMessageDeliveryStatus =
230: 	| "pending"

232: 	| "delivered"
233: 	| "rejected";
234: 
235: export interface UserChatMessage extends ChatMessageBase {

237: 	content: string;
238: 	/** True if this was the first prompt; false for follow-ups. */
239: 	isInitialPrompt: boolean;

380: export interface AgentChatSession {
381: 	/** UUIDv4 assigned at creation. Primary key. */
382: 	id: string;
383: 	source: "acp" | "cloud";
384: 	/** Reference to the underlying agent (ACP descriptor id or Cloud provider id). */
385: 	agentId: string;
386: 	/** Display name snapshotted at session start so UI stays stable if the catalog changes. */
387: 	agentDisplayName: string;
388: 	capabilities: ResolvedCapabilities;
389: 	/** Current mode id (undefined when no mode selector is shown). */
390: 	selectedModeId?: string;
391: 	/** Current model id (undefined when no model selector is shown). */
392: 	selectedModelId?: string;
393: 	/**
394: 	 * Models reported by the agent for this specific session via the
395: 	 * ACP `NewSessionResponse.models.availableModels` payload. Empty
396: 	 * array (or undefined for legacy sessions) means the agent did not
397: 	 * surface dynamic models — the picker falls back to the static
398: 	 * catalogue from `agent-capabilities-catalog.ts`.
399: 	 */
400: 	availableModels?: ModelDescriptor[];
401: 	/**
402: 	 * Currently selected model as last reported by the agent (via
403: 	 * `NewSessionResponse.models.currentModelId` or after a successful
404: 	 * `session/set_model` round-trip). `undefined` for sessions whose
405: 	 * agent does not implement the experimental capability.
406: 	 */
407: 	currentModelId?: string;
408: 	/** User-picked thinking level (e.g. "high"). Optional per provider. */
409: 	selectedThinkingLevelId?: string;
410: 	/** User-picked agent role (e.g. "agent" / "plan"). Optional per provider. */
411: 	selectedAgentRoleId?: string;
412: 	/**
413: 	 * Thinking levels surfaced by this provider for this session,
414: 	 * mirroring `availableModels`. When omitted/empty, the chip stays
415: 	 * hidden. Filled from `capabilities.thinkingLevels` at session
416: 	 * creation and refreshed if the agent updates them mid-session.
417: 	 */
418: 	availableThinkingLevels?: ThinkingLevelDescriptor[];
419: 	/** Same as above, for the agent-role picker. */
420: 	availableAgentRoles?: AgentRoleDescriptor[];
421: 	executionTarget: ExecutionTarget;
422: 	lifecycleState: SessionLifecycleState;
423: 	trigger: SessionTrigger;
424: 	/** Present when `executionTarget.kind === "worktree"`; null otherwise. */
425: 	worktree: WorktreeHandle | null;
426: 	/** Present when `source === "cloud"`; null otherwise. */
427: 	cloud: CloudLinkage | null;
428: 	createdAt: number;
429: 	updatedAt: number;
430: 	endedAt?: number;
431: 	/** VS Code workspace folder uri (for scoping). */
432: 	workspaceUri: string;
433: }
434: 
435: // ============================================================================
436: // Runtime event stream (runner -> panel/store)

459: 	  }

464: 			title?: string;

467: 	| {

471: 			status: ToolCallStatus;

473: 	  }
474: 	| {

478: 			to: SessionLifecycleState;
479: 			at: number;
480: 	  }
481: 	| {

486: 			retryable: boolean;

502: 
503: /**
504:  * Light projection of a queued ACP `writeTextFile` call. The webview

511: export interface PendingWriteSummary {

519: // ============================================================================

522:

524:  * Forward-compatible runner interface so `AgentChatRegistry` can be typed
525:  * during Phase 2 before the concrete `AcpChatRunner` (T027) and

1: /**
2:  * Cloud Agent Type Definitions
3:  *
4:  * Provider-agnostic types for the multi-provider cloud agent system.
5:  * These types are used across all cloud agent modules.
6:  *
7:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/data-model.md
8:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/provider-interface.md
9:  */
10: 
11: // ============================================================================
12: // Session Status
13: // ============================================================================
14: 
15: /**
16:  * Canonical session status values shared across all providers
17:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/data-model.md:L79-L88
18:  */
19: export const SessionStatus = {
20: 	PENDING: "pending",
21: 	RUNNING: "running",
22: 	BLOCKED: "blocked",
23: 	COMPLETED: "completed",
24: 	FAILED: "failed",
25: 	CANCELLED: "cancelled",
26: } as const;
27: export type SessionStatus = (typeof SessionStatus)[keyof typeof SessionStatus];
28: 
29: // ============================================================================
30: // Task Status
31: // ============================================================================
32: 
33: /**
34:  * Canonical task status values shared across all providers
35:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/data-model.md:L92-L98
36:  */
37: export const TaskStatus = {
38: 	PENDING: "pending",
39: 	IN_PROGRESS: "in_progress",
40: 	COMPLETED: "completed",
41: 	FAILED: "failed",
42: 	SKIPPED: "skipped",
43: } as const;
44: export type TaskStatus = (typeof TaskStatus)[keyof typeof TaskStatus];
45: 
46: // ============================================================================
47: // Task Priority
48: // ============================================================================
49: 
50: /**
51:  * Task priority levels
52:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/provider-interface.md
53:  */
54: export const TaskPriority = {
55: 	LOW: "low",
56: 	MEDIUM: "medium",
57: 	HIGH: "high",
58: 	CRITICAL: "critical",
59: } as const;
60: export type TaskPriority = (typeof TaskPriority)[keyof typeof TaskPriority];
61: 
62: // ============================================================================
63: // Provider Metadata
64: // ============================================================================
65: 
66: /**
67:  * Provider metadata and capability information.
68:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/provider-interface.md
69:  */
70: export interface ProviderMetadata {
71: 	/** Unique provider identifier (kebab-case, e.g., "devin", "github-copilot") */
72: 	readonly id: string;
73: 	/** Human-readable name displayed in UI */
74: 	readonly displayName: string;
75: 	/** Brief description for provider selection UI */
76: 	readonly description: string;
77: 	/** Icon identifier for tree view rendering */
78: 	readonly icon: string;
79: }
80: 
81: // ============================================================================
82: // Agent Session
83: // ============================================================================
84: 
85: /**
86:  * A unit of work dispatched to a cloud agent provider.

34:  * consumers (e.g. spec 018's cloud-chat-adapter) use it to drive chat-panel

56: // ============================================================================

60: /**
61:  * Polling orchestration that delegates to the active provider.

63:  *

65:  */
66: export class AgentPollingService {
67: 	private readonly registry: ProviderRegistry;
68: 	private readonly sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage;
69: 	private intervalId: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | undefined;
70: 	private consecutiveFailures = 0;
71: 	private readonly callbacks = {
72: 		onCredentialExpiry: undefined as (() => void) | undefined,
73: 		onSessionCompleted: undefined as
74: 			| ((localId: string, specPath: string) => void)
75: 			| undefined,
76: 		onError: undefined as ((message: string) => void) | undefined,
77: 		onUpdated: undefined as (() => void) | undefined,

80: 	/**

82: 	 * Additive to the existing single-callback `onUpdated` hook — both fire on
83: 	 * the same commit path.
84: 	 */
85: 	private readonly sessionUpdatedListeners =
86: 		new Set<AgentSessionUpdatedListener>();
87: 
88: 	/**

90: 	 */
91: 	get isRunning(): boolean {
92: 		return this.intervalId !== undefined;
93: 	}

96: 		this.registry = registry;
97: 		this.sessionStorage = sessionStorage;
98: 	}

100: 	/**

113: 	}
114: 
115: 	/**

117: 	 * Used to refresh the tree view UI.
118: 	 */
119: 	setOnUpdated(handler: () => void): void {
120: 		this.callbacks.onUpdated = handler;
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	/**
124: 	 * Register a callback for session completion events (FR-017).

126: 	 */
127: 	setOnSessionCompleted(
128: 		handler: (localId: string, specPath: string) => void
129: 	): void {
130: 		this.callbacks.onSessionCompleted = handler;
131: 	}
132: 
133: 	/**

182: 	}
183:

188: 	start(intervalMs: number): void {

207: 			this.intervalId = undefined;
208: 			logDebug("Polling stopped");
209: 		}
210: 	}

220: 		const now = Date.now();

222: 		const gracePeriodSessions: AgentSession[] = [];

224: 		for (const session of all) {
225: 			if (activeIds.has(session.localId)) {
226: 				continue;
227: 			}
228: 			if (session.isReadOnly) {
229: 				continue;
230: 			}
231: 			const completedAt = session.completedAt ?? session.updatedAt;
232: 			const hasUnknownPrState = session.pullRequests.some((pr) => !pr.state);
233: 			const effectiveGracePeriod = hasUnknownPrState
234: 				? PR_UNKNOWN_STATE_GRACE_PERIOD_MS
235: 				: PR_GRACE_PERIOD_MS;
236: 			if (now - completedAt >= effectiveGracePeriod) {
237: 				continue;
238: 			}
239: 			if (session.pullRequests.length === 0) {
240: 				continue;
241: 			}
242: 			if (
243: 				session.pullRequests.every((pr) =>
244: 					TERMINAL_PR_STATES.has(pr.state ?? "")
245: 				)
246: 			) {
247: 				continue;
248: 			}
249: 			gracePeriodSessions.push(session);
250: 		}
251: 
252: 		return [...active, ...gracePeriodSessions];
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	private async applyUpdate(update: SessionUpdate): Promise<void> {
256: 		const { localId, timestamp: _timestamp, ...fields } = update;
257: 		const isTerminal =
258: 			update.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED ||
259: 			update.status === SessionStatus.FAILED ||
260: 			update.status === SessionStatus.CANCELLED;
261: 
262: 		let derivedTasks: AgentTask[] | undefined;
263: 		if (isTerminal && !update.tasks) {
264: 			const current = await this.sessionStorage.getById(localId);
265: 			if (current && current.tasks.length > 0) {
266: 				derivedTasks = deriveTerminalTaskStatuses(current.tasks, update.status);
267: 			}
268: 		}
269: 
270: 		await this.sessionStorage.update(localId, {
271: 			...fields,
272: 			...(derivedTasks ? { tasks: derivedTasks } : {}),
273: 			...(isTerminal ? { completedAt: Date.now() } : {}),
274: 		});
275: 
276: 		// Fan out to spec 018 subscribers AFTER the commit so readers see the
277: 		// new state. Non-terminal updates also fire so the chat panel can
278: 		// reflect incremental status/blocked transitions.
279: 		if (this.sessionUpdatedListeners.size > 0) {
280: 			const snapshot = await this.sessionStorage.getById(localId);
281: 			if (snapshot) {
282: 				this.fireSessionUpdated({ localId, session: snapshot });
283: 			}
284: 		}
285: 
286: 		if (isTerminal && update.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED) {
287: 			const session = await this.sessionStorage.getById(localId);
288: 			if (session) {
289: 				this.callbacks.onSessionCompleted?.(localId, session.specPath);
290: 			}
291: 		}
292: 	}
293: 
294: 	private fireSessionUpdated(event: AgentSessionUpdatedEvent): void {
295: 		// Snapshot so listeners that dispose during delivery don't mutate the
296: 		// iteration.
297: 		for (const listener of [...this.sessionUpdatedListeners]) {
298: 			try {
299: 				listener(event);
300: 			} catch (error) {
301: 				logError("onSessionUpdated listener threw", error);
302: 			}
303: 		}
304: 	}
305: 
306: 	private handlePollError(error: unknown): void {
307: 		this.consecutiveFailures += 1;
308: 
309: 		if (this.isCredentialError(error)) {
310: 			logWarn("Credential expiry detected during polling");
311: 			this.callbacks.onCredentialExpiry?.();
312: 			return;
313: 		}
314: 
315: 		logError("Polling cycle failed", error);
316: 
317: 		const errorMsg =
318: 			error instanceof ProviderError
319: 				? error.message
320: 				: "Cloud agent polling failed. Check the output channel for details.";
321: 		this.callbacks.onError?.(errorMsg);
322: 
323: 		if (this.consecutiveFailures >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES) {
324: 			logWarn(
325: 				`Polling paused after ${MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES} consecutive failures`
326: 			);
327: 			this.stop();
328: 		}
329: 	}
330: 
331: 	private isCredentialError(error: unknown): boolean {
332: 		if (error instanceof ProviderError) {
333: 			return (
334: 				error.code === ErrorCode.CREDENTIALS_INVALID ||
335: 				error.code === ErrorCode.CREDENTIALS_MISSING
336: 			);
337: 		}
338: 		return false;
339: 	}
340: }
341: 
342: // ============================================================================
343: // Helpers
344: // ============================================================================
345: 
346: function deriveTerminalTaskStatuses(
347: 	tasks: readonly AgentTask[],
348: 	sessionStatus: SessionStatus | undefined
349: ): AgentTask[] {
350: 	let targetStatus: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.SKIPPED;
351: 	if (sessionStatus === SessionStatus.COMPLETED) {
352: 		targetStatus = TaskStatus.COMPLETED;
353: 	} else if (sessionStatus === SessionStatus.FAILED) {
354: 		targetStatus = TaskStatus.FAILED;
355: 	}
356: 
357: 	const now = Date.now();
358: 	return tasks.map((task) => {
359: 		if (
360: 			task.status === TaskStatus.COMPLETED ||
361: 			task.status === TaskStatus.FAILED ||
362: 			task.status === TaskStatus.SKIPPED
363: 		) {
364: 			return task;
365: 		}
366: 		return { ...task, status: targetStatus, completedAt: now };
367: 	});
368: }

parts.text:
**Planning implementation phase**

I need to implement phase 01 and conduct a thorough inspection. It seems like reusing a webview might be a quick fix. Adding a new webview panel in the main Gatomia views could be easiest, perhaps in `gatomia.views.orchestration` in package.json. I'll need to use apply_patch for edits only and create documentation files. Testing is necessary too, so I’ll inspect the existing test structures and the UI app root for page routing as well. I need to understand the webview bootstrapping process, so I'll search the ui/src/main for the page renderer.

I’ve confirmed there isn’t an existing orchestration surface to extend directly. I’m reading the webview app bootstrap and the tree/provider tests now so I can add one new page, one read-model service, and one provider with minimal cross-cutting changes.

1: /**
2:  * RunningAgentsTreeProvider unit tests (T042, spec 018).
3:  *
4:  * TDD (Constitution III): red before T044.
5:  *
6:  * Contract coverage:
7:  *   - Root level emits three groups ("Active", "Recent", "Orphaned worktrees").
8:  *   - Active group lists sessions with non-terminal lifecycle state.
9:  *   - Recent group lists sessions with terminal lifecycle state.
10:  *   - Leaves carry `agent · mode · target · status` metadata + a click command
11:  *     targeting `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession`.
12:  *   - Worktree-backed sessions carry a `*-worktree` contextValue so the
13:  *     view/item/context menu can expose "Clean up worktree".
14:  *   - The provider refreshes when `AgentChatSessionStore.onDidChangeManifest`
15:  *     or `AgentChatRegistry.onDidChange` fires.
16:  */
17: 
18: import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
19: import { EventEmitter, TreeItemCollapsibleState } from "vscode";
20: import type { AgentChatRegistry } from "../../../src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";
21: import type { AgentChatSessionStore } from "../../../src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store";
22: import {
23: 	RunningAgentsTreeProvider,
24: 	type RunningAgentsTreeItem,
25: } from "../../../src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider";
26: import type {
27: 	AgentChatSession,
28: 	OrphanedWorktreeEntry,
29: 	SessionManifest,
30: } from "../../../src/features/agent-chat/types";
31: 
32: // Top-level regexes (biome lint/performance/useTopLevelRegex).
33: const MODE_RE = /code/;
34: const TARGET_RE = /local/;
35: const STATUS_RE = /waiting-for-input/;
36: const PATH_RE = /tmp\/worktree-1/;
37: const BLOCKED_RE = /\(blocked\)/;
38: 
39: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40: // Fakes
41: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42: 
43: function makeSession(
44: 	overrides: Partial<AgentChatSession> = {}
45: ): AgentChatSession {
46: 	return {
47: 		id: "session-a",
48: 		source: "acp",
49: 		agentId: "opencode",
50: 		agentDisplayName: "OpenCode",
51: 		capabilities: { source: "none" },
52: 		executionTarget: { kind: "local" },
53: 		lifecycleState: "running",
54: 		trigger: { kind: "user" },
55: 		worktree: null,
56: 		cloud: null,
57: 		createdAt: 1000,
58: 		updatedAt: 1000,
59: 		workspaceUri: "file:///fake/workspace",
60: 		...overrides,
61: 	};
62: }
63: 
64: interface FakeStore {
65: 	manifestEmitter: EventEmitter<SessionManifest>;
66: 	active: AgentChatSession[];
67: 	recent: AgentChatSession[];
68: 	orphans: OrphanedWorktreeEntry[];
69: 	listActive(): Promise<AgentChatSession[]>;
70: 	listRecent(): Promise<AgentChatSession[]>;
71: 	listOrphanedWorktrees(): Promise<OrphanedWorktreeEntry[]>;
72: }
73: 
74: function createFakeStore(): FakeStore {
75: 	const manifestEmitter = new EventEmitter<SessionManifest>();
76: 	const fake: FakeStore = {
77: 		manifestEmitter,
78: 		active: [],
79: 		recent: [],
80: 		orphans: [],
81: 		listActive: () => Promise.resolve(fake.active),
82: 		listRecent: () => Promise.resolve(fake.recent),
83: 		listOrphanedWorktrees: () => Promise.resolve(fake.orphans),
84: 	};
85: 	return fake;
86: }
87: 
88: interface FakeRegistry {
89: 	registryEmitter: EventEmitter<void>;
90: 	onDidChange: EventEmitter<void>["event"];
91: }
92: 
93: function createFakeRegistry(): FakeRegistry {
94: 	const emitter = new EventEmitter<void>();
95: 	return {
96: 		registryEmitter: emitter,
97: 		onDidChange: emitter.event,
98: 	};
99: }
100: 
101: function assertGroupShape(
102: 	item: RunningAgentsTreeItem,
103: 	expectedLabel: string,
104: 	expectedContextValue: string
105: ): { labelOk: boolean; stateOk: boolean; contextOk: boolean } {
106: 	return {
107: 		labelOk: item.label === expectedLabel,
108: 		stateOk: item.collapsibleState === TreeItemCollapsibleState.Expanded,
109: 		contextOk: item.contextValue === expectedContextValue,
110: 	};
111: }
112: 
113: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
114: // Suite
115: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
116: 
117: describe("RunningAgentsTreeProvider (T042)", () => {
118: 	let store: FakeStore;
119: 	let registry: FakeRegistry;
120: 	let provider: RunningAgentsTreeProvider;
121: 
122: 	beforeEach(() => {
123: 		store = createFakeStore();
124: 		registry = createFakeRegistry();
125: 		provider = new RunningAgentsTreeProvider({
126: 			store: store as unknown as AgentChatSessionStore,
127: 			registry: registry as unknown as AgentChatRegistry,
128: 			storeChangeEvent: store.manifestEmitter.event,
129: 		});
130: 	});
131: 
132: 	describe("root groups", () => {
133: 		it("emits the New session leaf followed by three groups: Active, Recent, Orphaned worktrees", async () => {
134: 			const children = await provider.getChildren();
135: 			expect(children.map((c: RunningAgentsTreeItem) => c.label)).toEqual([
136: 				"+ New agent session…",
137: 				"Active",
138: 				"Recent",
139: 				"Orphaned worktrees",
140: 			]);
141: 		});
142: 
143: 		it("the New session leaf has a click command targeting gatomia.agentChat.newSession", async () => {
144: 			const children = await provider.getChildren();
145: 			const leaf = children[0];
146: 			expect(leaf.nodeKind).toBe("new-session-leaf");
147: 			expect(leaf.command).toMatchObject({
148: 				command: "gatomia.agentChat.newSession",
149: 			});
150: 		});
151: 
152: 		it("legacy: groups still match their original shape (label/contextValue/state)", async () => {
153: 			const children = await provider.getChildren();
154: 			const [, ...groups] = children;
155: 			expect(groups.map((c: RunningAgentsTreeItem) => c.label)).toEqual([
156: 				"Active",
157: 				"Recent",
158: 				"Orphaned worktrees",
159: 			]);
160: 
161: 			const active = assertGroupShape(
162: 				children[1],
163: 				"Active",
164: 				"agent-chat-group-active"
165: 			);
166: 			const recent = assertGroupShape(
167: 				children[2],
168: 				"Recent",
169: 				"agent-chat-group-recent"
170: 			);
171: 			const orphans = assertGroupShape(
172: 				children[3],
173: 				"Orphaned worktrees",
174: 				"agent-chat-group-orphans"
175: 			);
176: 			expect(active).toEqual({
177: 				labelOk: true,
178: 				stateOk: true,
179: 				contextOk: true,
180: 			});
181: 			expect(recent).toEqual({
182: 				labelOk: true,
183: 				stateOk: true,
184: 				contextOk: true,
185: 			});
186: 			expect(orphans).toEqual({
187: 				labelOk: true,
188: 				stateOk: true,
189: 				contextOk: true,
190: 			});
191: 		});
192: 	});
193: 
194: 	describe("session leaves", () => {
195: 		it("puts non-terminal sessions under Active and terminal ones under Recent", async () => {
196: 			const running = makeSession({
197: 				id: "s-run",
198: 				lifecycleState: "running",
199: 				selectedModeId: "code",
200: 			});
201: 			const done = makeSession({
202: 				id: "s-done",
203: 				lifecycleState: "completed",
204: 			});
205: 			store.active = [running];
206: 			store.recent = [done];
207: 
208: 			const [, activeGroup, recentGroup] = await provider.getChildren();
209: 			const activeChildren = await provider.getChildren(activeGroup);
210: 			const recentChildren = await provider.getChildren(recentGroup);
211: 
212: 			expect(
213: 				activeChildren.map((c: RunningAgentsTreeItem) => c.sessionId)
214: 			).toEqual(["s-run"]);
215: 			expect(
216: 				recentChildren.map((c: RunningAgentsTreeItem) => c.sessionId)
217: 			).toEqual(["s-done"]);
218: 		});
219: 
220: 		it("renders leaves with 'agent · mode · target · status' in the description", async () => {
221: 			const s = makeSession({
222: 				id: "s-desc",
223: 				selectedModeId: "code",
224: 				lifecycleState: "waiting-for-input",
225: 				executionTarget: { kind: "local" },
226: 			});
227: 			store.active = [s];
228: 
229: 			const [, activeGroup] = await provider.getChildren();
230: 			const [leaf] = await provider.getChildren(activeGroup);
231: 
232: 			// Label is the agent display name; description carries the metadata.
233: 			expect(leaf.label).toBe("OpenCode");
234: 			expect(leaf.description).toMatch(MODE_RE);
235: 			expect(leaf.description).toMatch(TARGET_RE);
236: 			expect(leaf.description).toMatch(STATUS_RE);
237: 		});
238: 
239: 		it("attaches a click command targeting gatomia.agentChat.openForSession with the session id", async () => {
240: 			const s = makeSession({ id: "s-cmd" });
241: 			store.active = [s];
242: 
243: 			const [, activeGroup] = await provider.getChildren();
244: 			const [leaf] = await provider.getChildren(activeGroup);
245: 
246: 			expect(leaf.command).toMatchObject({
247: 				command: "gatomia.agentChat.openForSession",
248: 				arguments: ["s-cmd"],
249: 			});
250: 		});
251: 
252: 		it("uses a worktree-specific contextValue for worktree-backed sessions", async () => {
253: 			const worktreeSession = makeSession({
254: 				id: "s-wt",
255: 				executionTarget: { kind: "worktree", worktreeId: "wt-1" },
256: 				worktree: {
257: 					id: "wt-1",
258: 					absolutePath: "/tmp/worktree",
259: 					branchName: "gatomia/agent-chat/s-wt",

262: 					status: "in-use",
263: 				},
264: 			});
265: 			store.active = [worktreeSession];
266: 
267: 			const [, activeGroup] = await provider.getChildren();
268: 			const [leaf] = await provider.getChildren(activeGroup);
269: 
270: 			expect(leaf.contextValue).toBe("agent-chat-session-worktree");
271: 		});
272: 
273: 		it("uses the plain session contextValue for local sessions", async () => {
274: 			store.active = [makeSession({ id: "s-local" })];
275: 			const [, activeGroup] = await provider.getChildren();
276: 			const [leaf] = await provider.getChildren(activeGroup);
277: 			expect(leaf.contextValue).toBe("agent-chat-session");
278: 		});
279: 	});
280: 
281: 	describe("orphaned worktrees group", () => {
282: 		it("renders each orphaned worktree entry as a leaf", async () => {
283: 			store.orphans = [
284: 				{
285: 					sessionId: "evicted-1",
286: 					absolutePath: "/tmp/worktree-1",
287: 					branchName: "gatomia/agent-chat/evicted-1",

289: 				},
290: 			];
291: 			const children = await provider.getChildren();

293: 			const orphanChildren = await provider.getChildren(orphanGroup);

295: 			expect(orphanChildren).toHaveLength(1);

297: 			expect(orphanChildren[0].description).toMatch(PATH_RE);
298: 		});
299: 	});
300: 
301: 	// ----- T071 (spec 018 US4) -----
302: 	describe("blocked flag for waiting-for-input sessions", () => {
303: 		it("suffixes '(blocked)' on the description when the session is waiting-for-input", async () => {
304: 			store.active = [
305: 				makeSession({
306: 					id: "s-blocked",
307: 					lifecycleState: "waiting-for-input",
308: 					selectedModeId: "code",
309: 				}),
310: 			];
311: 
312: 			const [, activeGroup] = await provider.getChildren();
313: 			const [leaf] = await provider.getChildren(activeGroup);
314: 
315: 			expect(leaf.description).toMatch(BLOCKED_RE);
316: 		});
317: 
318: 		it("does NOT suffix '(blocked)' on non-waiting sessions", async () => {
319: 			store.active = [
320: 				makeSession({
321: 					id: "s-running",
322: 					lifecycleState: "running",
323: 					selectedModeId: "code",
324: 				}),
325: 			];
326: 
327: 			const [, activeGroup] = await provider.getChildren();
328: 			const [leaf] = await provider.getChildren(activeGroup);
329: 
330: 			expect(String(leaf.description ?? "")).not.toMatch(BLOCKED_RE);
331: 		});
332: 
333: 		it("uses the 'bell' codicon for waiting-for-input sessions", async () => {
334: 			store.active = [
335: 				makeSession({
336: 					id: "s-blocked-icon",
337: 					lifecycleState: "waiting-for-input",
338: 				}),
339: 			];
340: 
341: 			const [, activeGroup] = await provider.getChildren();
342: 			const [leaf] = await provider.getChildren(activeGroup);
343: 
344: 			// ThemeIcon stores its id on the `id` property.
345: 			expect((leaf.iconPath as { id: string }).id).toBe("bell");
346: 		});
347: 	});
348: 
349: 	describe("reactivity", () => {
350: 		it("fires onDidChangeTreeData when the store manifest changes", () => {
351: 			const listener = vi.fn();
352: 			provider.onDidChangeTreeData(listener);
353: 			store.manifestEmitter.fire({
354: 				schemaVersion: 1,
355: 				sessions: [],
356: 				updatedAt: Date.now(),
357: 			});
358: 			expect(listener).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
359: 		});
360: 
361: 		it("fires onDidChangeTreeData when the registry changes", () => {
362: 			const listener = vi.fn();
363: 			provider.onDidChangeTreeData(listener);
364: 			registry.registryEmitter.fire();
365: 			expect(listener).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
366: 		});
367: 	});
368: 
369: 	describe("lifecycle", () => {
370: 		it("dispose stops forwarding store and registry events", () => {
371: 			const listener = vi.fn();
372: 			provider.onDidChangeTreeData(listener);
373: 			provider.dispose();

377: 				updatedAt: Date.now(),
378: 			});
379: 			registry.registryEmitter.fire();
380: 			expect(listener).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
381: 		});
382: 	});
383: });

/**
 * RunningAgentsTreeProvider unit tests (T042, spec 018).
 *
 * TDD (Constitution III): red before T044.
 *
 * Contract coverage:
 *   - Root level emits three groups ("Active", "Recent", "Orphaned worktrees").
 *   - Active group lists sessions with non-terminal lifecycle state.
 *   - Recent group lists sessions with terminal lifecycle state.
 *   - Leaves carry `agent · mode · target · status` metadata + a click command
 *     targeting `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession`.
 *   - Worktree-backed sessions carry a `*-worktree` contextValue so the
 *     view/item/context menu can expose "Clean up worktree".
 *   - The provider refreshes when `AgentChatSessionStore.onDidChangeManifest`
 *     or `AgentChatRegistry.onDidChange` fires.
 */

import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
import { EventEmitter, TreeItemCollapsibleState } from "vscode";
import type { AgentChatRegistry } from "../../../src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";

<path>/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/tests/unit/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.test.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * AgentChatViewProvider tests.
3:  *
4:  * Validates the canonical sidebar webview surface:
5:  *   - resolveWebviewView pushes catalog + session list eagerly
6:  *   - `agent-chat/control/switch-session` rebinds without leaking
7:  *     subscriptions
8:  *   - `agent-chat/control/request-new-chat` clears the binding and emits
9:  *     `session/cleared`
10:  *   - `agent-chat/control/new-session` dispatches the start-new command
11:  *     with the picked provider/model/agent-file
12:  *   - registry/store change events trigger session-list rebroadcast
13:  */
14: 
15: import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
16: import { commands, ConfigurationTarget, EventEmitter, workspace } from "vscode";
17: import { AgentChatViewProvider } from "../../../src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider";
18: import { AgentChatRegistry } from "../../../src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";
19: import {
20: 	AgentChatSessionStore,
21: 	type AgentChatArchiveWriter,
22: 	type AgentChatMemento,
23: } from "../../../src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store";
24: import type {
25: 	AgentChatSession,
26: 	ChatMessage,
27: } from "../../../src/features/agent-chat/types";
28: 
29: // ============================================================================
30: // Fakes
31: // ============================================================================
32: 
33: const NOOP = (): void => {
34: 	// Intentionally empty.
35: };
36: 
37: function createMockMemento(): AgentChatMemento & {
38: 	_store: Map<string, unknown>;
39: } {
40: 	const store = new Map<string, unknown>();
41: 	return {
42: 		_store: store,
43: 		get: <T>(key: string, defaultValue?: T): T | undefined =>
44: 			(store.has(key) ? store.get(key) : defaultValue) as T | undefined,
45: 		update: (key: string, value: unknown): Thenable<void> => {
46: 			if (value === undefined) {
47: 				store.delete(key);
48: 			} else {
49: 				store.set(key, value);
50: 			}
51: 			return Promise.resolve();
52: 		},
53: 		keys: (): readonly string[] => [...store.keys()],
54: 	};
55: }
56: 
57: function createMockArchive(): AgentChatArchiveWriter {
58: 	return {
59: 		appendLines: vi.fn(() => Promise.resolve("a.jsonl")) as (
60: 			sessionId: string,
61: 			messages: ChatMessage[]
62: 		) => Promise<string>,
63: 		readLines: vi.fn(() => Promise.resolve([])) as (
64: 			sessionId: string,
65: 			offset: number,
66: 			limit: number
67: 		) => Promise<ChatMessage[]>,
68: 	};
69: }
70: 
71: interface FakeWebview {
72: 	html: string;
73: 	options: unknown;
74: 	postMessage: ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>;
75: 	onDidReceiveMessage: ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>;
76: 	asWebviewUri: ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>;
77: 	cspSource: string;
78: 	_inject(message: unknown): void;
79: 	_listeners: Array<(msg: unknown) => void>;
80: }
81: 
82: interface FakeWebviewView {
83: 	webview: FakeWebview;
84: 	onDidDispose: (cb: () => void) => { dispose: () => void };
85: 	show: ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>;
86: 	_fireDispose(): void;
87: }
88: 
89: function createFakeView(): FakeWebviewView {
90: 	const listeners: Array<(msg: unknown) => void> = [];
91: 	const webview: FakeWebview = {
92: 		html: "",
93: 		options: {},
94: 		postMessage: vi.fn(() => Promise.resolve(true)),
95: 		onDidReceiveMessage: vi.fn((cb: (msg: unknown) => void) => {
96: 			listeners.push(cb);
97: 			return { dispose: NOOP };
98: 		}),
99: 		asWebviewUri: vi.fn((uri) => uri),
100: 		cspSource: "vscode-webview://test",
101: 		_inject: (m) => {
102: 			for (const cb of listeners) {
103: 				cb(m);
104: 			}
105: 		},
106: 		_listeners: listeners,
107: 	};
108: 	let disposeHandler: (() => void) | undefined;
109: 	return {
110: 		webview,
111: 		onDidDispose: (cb) => {
112: 			disposeHandler = cb;
113: 			return { dispose: NOOP };
114: 		},
115: 		show: vi.fn(),
116: 		_fireDispose: () => disposeHandler?.(),
117: 	};
118: }

**Planning implementation steps**

I've gathered enough context, so it's time to fully implement phase 01. It seems the user likely doesn't want actual code, so I might skip using TodoWrite and Task. Instead, I should focus on apply_patch for any edits. It would make sense to check existing code for similar helpers and inspect relevant files—maybe I can repurpose something. I also need to look into commands, provider registration patterns, and documentation. Getting a grasp on the existing UI architecture will help, especially for anything under ui/src/features/orchestration.

I have enough structure to start the design. I’m reading the React entrypoint and simple webview provider patterns now, then I’ll implement the normalized read model and wire a dedicated orchestration webview around it.

1: // Minimal wrapper around acquireVsCodeApi with a dev-friendly fallback
2: 
3: interface VsCodeApi {
4: 	postMessage: (message: any) => void;
5: 	getState: () => any;
6: 	setState: (state: any) => void;
7: }
8: 
9: declare global {
10: 	interface Window {
11: 		acquireVsCodeApi?: () => VsCodeApi;
12: 	}
13: }
14: 
15: const DEV_ECHO_TIMEOUT_MS = 50;
16: 
17: let vscodeApi: VsCodeApi;
18: if (
19: 	typeof window !== "undefined" &&
20: 	typeof window.acquireVsCodeApi === "function"
21: ) {
22: 	vscodeApi = window.acquireVsCodeApi!();
23: } else {
24: 	// Local preview fallback (no-op postMessage)
25: 	vscodeApi = {
26: 		postMessage: (msg: any) => {
27: 			// Simulate echo in dev: bounce back the same message shape
28: 			window.setTimeout(() => {
29: 				window.dispatchEvent(
30: 					new MessageEvent("message", {
31: 						data: {
32: 							type: "openspec.chat/echoResult",
33: 							id: msg.id,
34: 							text: msg.text,
35: 							ts: Date.now(),
36: 						},
37: 					})
38: 				);
39: 			}, DEV_ECHO_TIMEOUT_MS);
40: 		},
41: 		getState: () => ({}),
42: 		// In dev mode, state is not persisted
43: 		setState: () => ({}),
44: 	};
45: }
46: 
47: export const vscode = vscodeApi;

1: import {

13: interface QuickAccessGroupDefinition {

20: const GROUPS: QuickAccessGroupDefinition[] = [
21: 	{
22: 		id: "spec-system",

24: 		description: "Configure which specification system to use.",

35: 		label: "Resources",
36: 		description: "Help and documentation.",
37: 		icon: new ThemeIcon("book"),
38: 	},
39: ];
40: 
41: type TreeEventPayload = QuickAccessTreeItem | undefined | null | void;
42: 
43: export class QuickAccessExplorerProvider
44: 	implements TreeDataProvider<QuickAccessTreeItem>
45: {
46: 	static readonly viewId = "gatomia.views.overview";
47: 
48: 	private readonly changeEmitter = new EventEmitter<TreeEventPayload>();
49: 	readonly onDidChangeTreeData: Event<TreeEventPayload> =
50: 		this.changeEmitter.event;
51: 
52: 	private readonly configManager: ConfigManager;
53: 
54: 	constructor() {
55: 		this.configManager = ConfigManager.getInstance();
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	dispose(): void {
59: 		this.changeEmitter.dispose();
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	refresh(): void {
63: 		this.changeEmitter.fire();
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	getTreeItem(element: QuickAccessTreeItem): TreeItem {
67: 		return element;
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	getChildren(element?: QuickAccessTreeItem): QuickAccessTreeItem[] {
71: 		if (!element) {
72: 			return GROUPS.map((group) => QuickAccessTreeItem.createGroup(group));
73: 		}
74: 
75: 		if (element.contextValue === "quick-access-group") {
76: 			return this.getGroupChildren(element.groupId);
77: 		}
78: 
79: 		return [];
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	private getGroupChildren(groupId: string | undefined): QuickAccessTreeItem[] {
83: 		if (groupId === "spec-system") {
84: 			return this.getSpecSystemItems();
85: 		}
86: 
87: 		if (groupId === "configuration") {
88: 			return this.getConfigurationItems();
89: 		}
90: 
91: 		if (groupId === "resources") {
92: 			return this.getResourceItems();
93: 		}
94: 
95: 		return [];
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	private getSpecSystemItems(): QuickAccessTreeItem[] {
99: 		const currentSystem = this.configManager.getSettings().specSystem;
100: 		const systemLabel = this.formatSpecSystemLabel(currentSystem);
101: 
102: 		return [
103: 			QuickAccessTreeItem.createAction(
104: 				"Select Spec Agent",
105: 				{
106: 					command: "gatomia.settings.selectSpecSystem",
107: 					title: "Select Spec Agent",
108: 				},
109: 				new ThemeIcon("settings-gear"),
110: 				`Current: ${systemLabel}. Click to change the active spec system.`
111: 			),
112: 			QuickAccessTreeItem.createAction(
113: 				"Install Dependencies",
114: 				{
115: 					command: "gatomia.dependencies.check",
116: 					title: "Install Dependencies",
117: 				},
118: 				new ThemeIcon("desktop-download"),
119: 				"Check and install required dependencies for SpecKit and OpenSpec."
120: 			),
121: 		];
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	private getConfigurationItems(): QuickAccessTreeItem[] {
125: 		return [
126: 			QuickAccessTreeItem.createAction(
127: 				"Open Settings",
128: 				{
129: 					command: "gatomia.settings.open",
130: 					title: "Open Settings",
131: 				},
132: 				new ThemeIcon("gear"),
133: 				"Open GatomIA extension settings."
134: 			),
135: 			QuickAccessTreeItem.createAction(
136: 				"Open MCP Config",
137: 				{
138: 					command: "gatomia.settings.openGlobalConfig",
139: 					title: "Open MCP Config",
140: 				},
141: 				new ThemeIcon("mcp"),
142: 				"Open the MCP configuration file (mcp.json)."
143: 			),
144: 		];
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	private getResourceItems(): QuickAccessTreeItem[] {
148: 		return [
149: 			QuickAccessTreeItem.createAction(
150: 				"Help",
151: 				{
152: 					command: "gatomia.help.open",
153: 					title: "Help",
154: 				},
155: 				new ThemeIcon("question"),
156: 				"Open GatomIA documentation and help."
157: 			),
158: 		];
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	private formatSpecSystemLabel(system: string): string {
162: 		if (system === SPEC_SYSTEM_MODE.SPECKIT) {
163: 			return "SpecKit";
164: 		}
165: 		if (system === SPEC_SYSTEM_MODE.OPENSPEC) {
166: 			return "OpenSpec";
167: 		}
168: 		return "Auto-detect";
169: 	}
170: }
171: 
172: export class QuickAccessTreeItem extends TreeItem {
173: 	groupId?: string;
174: 
175: 	private constructor(
176: 		label: string,
177: 		collapsibleState: TreeItemCollapsibleState,
178: 		contextValue: string
179: 	) {
180: 		super(label, collapsibleState);
181: 		this.contextValue = contextValue;
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	static createGroup(
185: 		definition: QuickAccessGroupDefinition
186: 	): QuickAccessTreeItem {
187: 		const item = new QuickAccessTreeItem(
188: 			definition.label,
189: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.Expanded,
190: 			"quick-access-group"
191: 		);
192: 		item.iconPath = definition.icon;
193: 		item.tooltip = definition.description;
194: 		item.groupId = definition.id;
195: 		return item;
196: 	}
197: 
198: 	static createAction(
199: 		label: string,
200: 		command: Command,
201: 		icon: ThemeIcon,
202: 		tooltip?: string
203: 	): QuickAccessTreeItem {
204: 		const item = new QuickAccessTreeItem(
205: 			label,
206: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
207: 			"quick-access-action"
208: 		);
209: 		item.iconPath = icon;
210: 		item.command = command;
211: 		item.tooltip = tooltip;
212: 		return item;
213: 	}
214: }

1: /**
2:  * Provider Registry
3:  *
4:  * Manages registration and active-provider orchestration for cloud agent providers.
5:  * Supports registering adapters, getting/setting the active provider, and listing all providers.
6:  *
7:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/provider-interface.md
8:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/plan.md
9:  */
10: 
11: import type { CloudAgentProvider } from "./cloud-agent-provider";
12: import { logInfo, logError } from "./logging";
13: import type { ProviderConfigStore } from "./provider-config-store";
14: 
15: // ============================================================================
16: // ProviderRegistry
17: // ============================================================================
18: 
19: /**
20:  * Registry for cloud agent provider adapters.
21:  * Manages available providers and active provider state.
22:  *
23:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/provider-interface.md
24:  */
25: export class ProviderRegistry {
26: 	private readonly providers = new Map<string, CloudAgentProvider>();
27: 	private readonly configStore: ProviderConfigStore;
28: 	private activeProviderId: string | undefined;
29: 
30: 	constructor(configStore: ProviderConfigStore) {
31: 		this.configStore = configStore;
32: 	}
33: 
34: 	/**
35: 	 * Register a provider adapter. Throws if a provider with the same ID is already registered.
36: 	 * @param provider - The provider to register
37: 	 */
38: 	register(provider: CloudAgentProvider): void {
39: 		const id = provider.metadata.id;
40: 		if (this.providers.has(id)) {
41: 			throw new Error(`Provider "${id}" is already registered`);
42: 		}
43: 		this.providers.set(id, provider);
44: 		logInfo(`Provider registered: ${id}`);
45: 	}
46: 
47: 	/**
48: 	 * Get a registered provider by ID.
49: 	 * @param id - The provider ID
50: 	 * @returns The provider or undefined if not found
51: 	 */
52: 	get(id: string): CloudAgentProvider | undefined {
53: 		return this.providers.get(id);
54: 	}
55: 
56: 	/**
57: 	 * Get all registered providers.
58: 	 * @returns Array of all registered providers
59: 	 */
60: 	getAll(): CloudAgentProvider[] {
61: 		return [...this.providers.values()];
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	/**
65: 	 * Get the currently active provider.
66: 	 * @returns The active provider or undefined if none is set
67: 	 */
68: 	getActive(): CloudAgentProvider | undefined {
69: 		if (!this.activeProviderId) {
70: 			return;
71: 		}
72: 		return this.providers.get(this.activeProviderId);
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	/**
76: 	 * Set the active provider. Throws if the provider is not registered.
77: 	 * @param id - The provider ID to activate
78: 	 */
79: 	async setActive(id: string): Promise<void> {
80: 		if (!this.providers.has(id)) {
81: 			throw new Error(`Provider "${id}" is not registered`);
82: 		}
83: 		this.activeProviderId = id;
84: 		await this.configStore.setActiveProvider(id);
85: 		logInfo(`Active provider set to: ${id}`);
86: 	}
87: 
88: 	/**
89: 	 * Clear the active provider.
90: 	 */
91: 	async clearActive(): Promise<void> {
92: 		this.activeProviderId = undefined;
93: 		await this.configStore.clearActiveProvider();
94: 		logInfo("Active provider cleared");
95: 	}
96: 
97: 	/**
98: 	 * Restore the active provider from the config store.
99: 	 * Called on extension activation to resume state.
100: 	 */
101: 	async restoreActive(): Promise<void> {
102: 		const storedId = await this.configStore.getActiveProvider();
103: 		if (storedId && this.providers.has(storedId)) {
104: 			this.activeProviderId = storedId;
105: 			logInfo(`Active provider restored: ${storedId}`);
106: 		} else if (storedId) {
107: 			logError(`Stored active provider "${storedId}" not found in registry`);
108: 		}
109: 	}
110: }

(End of file - total 110 lines)
</content>
