<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * Agent Session Storage
3:  *
4:  * Provider-agnostic session CRUD and read-only marking.
5:  * Stores sessions in VS Code workspace state.
6:  *
7:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/storage-interface.md
8:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/data-model.md
9:  */
10: 
11: import { logInfo, logDebug } from "./logging";
12: import type { Memento } from "./provider-config-store";
13: import {
14: 	SessionStatus,
15: 	TaskStatus,
16: 	type AgentSession,
17: 	type AgentTask,
18: 	type PullRequest,
19: 	StoreError,
20: 	StoreErrorCode,
21: } from "./types";
22: 
23: // ============================================================================
24: // Storage Key
25: // ============================================================================
26: 
27: const SESSIONS_KEY = "gatomia.cloudAgent.sessions";
28: 
29: // ============================================================================
30: // AgentSessionStorage
31: // ============================================================================
32: 
33: /**
34:  * Provider-agnostic session storage using VS Code workspace state.
35:  *
36:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/storage-interface.md
37:  */
38: export class AgentSessionStorage {
39: 	private readonly workspaceState: Memento;
40: 
41: 	constructor(workspaceState: Memento) {
42: 		this.workspaceState = workspaceState;
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	/**
46: 	 * Get all sessions for the current workspace.
47: 	 */
48: 	getAll(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
49: 		const raw = this.workspaceState.get<unknown[]>(SESSIONS_KEY);
50: 		if (!Array.isArray(raw)) {
51: 			return Promise.resolve([]);
52: 		}
53: 		const valid: AgentSession[] = [];
54: 		for (const entry of raw) {
55: 			if (isValidAgentSession(entry)) {
56: 				valid.push(normalizeSession(entry as AgentSession));
57: 			} else {
58: 				logDebug(
59: 					`Skipping invalid session entry: ${JSON.stringify(entry).slice(0, 200)}`
60: 				);
61: 			}
62: 		}
63: 		return Promise.resolve(valid);
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	/**
67: 	 * Get a single session by local ID.
68: 	 */
69: 	async getById(localId: string): Promise<AgentSession | undefined> {
70: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
71: 		return sessions.find((s) => s.localId === localId);
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	/**
75: 	 * Get sessions filtered by provider ID.
76: 	 */
77: 	async getByProvider(providerId: string): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
78: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
79: 		return sessions.filter((s) => s.providerId === providerId);
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	/**
83: 	 * Get active (non-terminal, non-read-only) sessions.
84: 	 */
85: 	async getActive(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
86: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
87: 		return sessions.filter((s) => {
88: 			if (s.isReadOnly) {
89: 				return false;
90: 			}
91: 			switch (s.status) {
92: 				case SessionStatus.COMPLETED:
93: 				case SessionStatus.FAILED:
94: 				case SessionStatus.CANCELLED:
95: 					return false;
96: 				default:
97: 					return true;
98: 			}
99: 		});
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	/**
103: 	 * Check if a task (by specTaskId) is already in a non-terminal session.
104: 	 * Returns the running session if found, undefined otherwise.
105: 	 */
106: 	async findActiveBySpecTaskId(
107: 		specTaskId: string
108: 	): Promise<AgentSession | undefined> {
109: 		const active = await this.getActive();
110: 		return active.find((s) => s.tasks.some((t) => t.specTaskId === specTaskId));
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	/**
114: 	 * Save a new session.
115: 	 * @throws StoreError if localId already exists
116: 	 */
117: 	async create(session: AgentSession): Promise<void> {
118: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
119: 		if (sessions.some((s) => s.localId === session.localId)) {
120: 			throw new StoreError(
121: 				`Session "${session.localId}" already exists`,
122: 				StoreErrorCode.ALREADY_EXISTS,
123: 				"create"
124: 			);
125: 		}
126: 		sessions.push(session);
127: 		await this.persist(sessions);
128: 		logInfo(`Session created: ${session.localId} (${session.providerId})`);
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	/**
132: 	 * Update an existing session.
133: 	 * @throws StoreError if session not found
134: 	 */
135: 	async update(localId: string, updates: Partial<AgentSession>): Promise<void> {
136: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
137: 		const index = sessions.findIndex((s) => s.localId === localId);
138: 		if (index === -1) {
139: 			throw new StoreError(
140: 				`Session "${localId}" not found`,
141: 				StoreErrorCode.NOT_FOUND,
142: 				"update"
143: 			);
144: 		}
145: 		const cleaned = stripUndefined(updates);
146: 		sessions[index] = { ...sessions[index], ...cleaned, updatedAt: Date.now() };
147: 		await this.persist(sessions);
148: 		logDebug(`Session updated: ${localId}`);
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	/**
152: 	 * Delete a session.
153: 	 */
154: 	async delete(localId: string): Promise<void> {
155: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
156: 		const filtered = sessions.filter((s) => s.localId !== localId);
157: 		await this.persist(filtered);
158: 		logInfo(`Session deleted: ${localId}`);
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	/**
162: 	 * Mark all sessions from a provider as read-only.
163: 	 */
164: 	async markProviderReadOnly(providerId: string): Promise<void> {
165: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
166: 		for (const session of sessions) {
167: 			if (session.providerId === providerId) {
168: 				session.isReadOnly = true;
169: 			}
170: 		}
171: 		await this.persist(sessions);
172: 		logInfo(`All sessions for provider "${providerId}" marked read-only`);
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	/**
176: 	 * Get terminal sessions that need cleanup (updated before cutoff time).
177: 	 * Only returns sessions in completed/failed/cancelled state.
178: 	 */
179: 	async getForCleanup(cutoffTime: number): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
180: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
181: 		return sessions.filter((s) => {
182: 			const isTerminal =
183: 				s.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED ||
184: 				s.status === SessionStatus.FAILED ||
185: 				s.status === SessionStatus.CANCELLED;
186: 			if (!isTerminal) {
187: 				return false;
188: 			}
189: 			const completionTime = s.completedAt ?? s.updatedAt;
190: 			return completionTime < cutoffTime;
191: 		});
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	private async persist(sessions: AgentSession[]): Promise<void> {
195: 		await this.workspaceState.update(SESSIONS_KEY, sessions);
196: 	}
197: }
198: 
199: // ============================================================================
200: // Validation
201: // ============================================================================
202: 
203: /**
204:  * Runtime check that a deserialized value has the minimum required shape of an AgentSession.
205:  * Filters out corrupted or schema-incompatible entries after deserialization.
206:  */
207: /**
208:  * Remove undefined values from an object to prevent overwriting
209:  * existing fields (especially arrays like tasks/pullRequests) with undefined
210:  * during a partial update spread.
211:  */
212: function stripUndefined<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(obj: T): Partial<T> {
213: 	const result: Record<string, unknown> = {};
214: 	for (const key of Object.keys(obj)) {
215: 		if (obj[key] !== undefined) {
216: 			result[key] = obj[key];
217: 		}
218: 	}
219: 	return result as Partial<T>;
220: }
221: 
222: const TERMINAL_SESSION_STATUSES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
223: 	SessionStatus.COMPLETED,
224: 	SessionStatus.FAILED,
225: 	SessionStatus.CANCELLED,
226: ]);
227: 
228: const TERMINAL_TASK_STATUSES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
229: 	TaskStatus.COMPLETED,
230: 	TaskStatus.FAILED,
231: 	TaskStatus.SKIPPED,
232: ]);
233: 
234: const SESSION_TO_TASK_STATUS: Record<string, TaskStatus> = {
235: 	[SessionStatus.COMPLETED]: TaskStatus.COMPLETED,
236: 	[SessionStatus.FAILED]: TaskStatus.FAILED,
237: 	[SessionStatus.CANCELLED]: TaskStatus.SKIPPED,
238: };
239: 
240: function normalizeSession(session: AgentSession): AgentSession {
241: 	if (!TERMINAL_SESSION_STATUSES.has(session.status)) {
242: 		return session;
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	let tasks: AgentTask[] | undefined;
246: 	const hasStaleTask = session.tasks.some(
247: 		(t) => !TERMINAL_TASK_STATUSES.has(t.status)
248: 	);
249: 	if (hasStaleTask) {
250: 		const targetStatus =
251: 			SESSION_TO_TASK_STATUS[session.status] ?? TaskStatus.SKIPPED;
252: 		const now = Date.now();
253: 		tasks = session.tasks.map((task) => {
254: 			if (TERMINAL_TASK_STATUSES.has(task.status)) {
255: 				return task;
256: 			}
257: 			return { ...task, status: targetStatus, completedAt: now };
258: 		});
259: 	}
260: 
261: 	let pullRequests: PullRequest[] | undefined;
262: 	const hasUnknownPrState = session.pullRequests.some((pr) => !pr.state);
263: 	if (hasUnknownPrState) {
264: 		const defaultState =
265: 			session.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED ? "merged" : "open";
266: 		pullRequests = session.pullRequests.map((pr) =>
267: 			pr.state ? pr : { ...pr, state: defaultState }
268: 		);
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	if (tasks || pullRequests) {
272: 		return {
273: 			...session,
274: 			...(tasks ? { tasks } : {}),
275: 			...(pullRequests ? { pullRequests } : {}),
276: 		};
277: 	}
278: 	return session;
279: }
280: 
281: function isValidAgentSession(value: unknown): boolean {
282: 	if (typeof value !== "object" || value === null) {
283: 		return false;
284: 	}
285: 	const obj = value as Record<string, unknown>;
286: 	return (
287: 		typeof obj.localId === "string" &&
288: 		typeof obj.providerId === "string" &&
289: 		typeof obj.status === "string" &&
290: 		typeof obj.branch === "string" &&
291: 		typeof obj.specPath === "string" &&
292: 		typeof obj.createdAt === "number" &&
293: 		typeof obj.updatedAt === "number" &&
294: 		Array.isArray(obj.tasks) &&
295: 		Array.isArray(obj.pullRequests)
296: 	);
297: }

(End of file - total 297 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * AgentChatRegistry — in-memory index of live chat sessions, panels, and
3:  * runners for the Agent Chat Panel feature.
4:  *
5:  * Responsibilities:
6:  *   - Track one `WebviewPanel` per session (FR-008 invariant).
7:  *   - Track the active runner (`AcpChatRunner` or `CloudChatAdapter`) for each
8:  *     session via the forward-compatible `AgentChatRunnerHandle` interface.
9:  *   - Partition sessions into active vs. recent buckets for the tree view.
10:  *   - Drive the shutdown flush handed to `AgentChatSessionStore`.
11:  *
12:  * Persistence is out of scope for this module — the store owns disk state.
13:  * The registry is the authority on "what is live in this process right now".
14:  *
15:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/contracts/agent-chat-panel-protocol.md §2
16:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/data-model.md §5 (Invariants)
17:  */
18: 
19: import { EventEmitter } from "vscode";
20: import {
21: 	type AgentChatRunnerHandle,
22: 	type AgentChatSession,
23: 	TERMINAL_STATES,
24: } from "./types";
25: 
26: // ============================================================================
27: // T073 — concurrent-cap enforcement
28: // ============================================================================
29: 
30: /**
31:  * Result of {@link AgentChatRegistry.checkCapacity}. `ok: true` means the
32:  * caller may proceed to spawn a new session. `ok: false` carries the idle
33:  * session list so the UX layer (T073a) can build a QuickPick.
34:  */
35: export type CapacityCheck =
36: 	| { readonly ok: true }
37: 	| {
38: 			readonly ok: false;
39: 			readonly idleSessions: readonly AgentChatSession[];
40: 			readonly cap: number;
41: 	  };
42: 
43: /**
44:  * Thrown by call-sites that prefer `try`/`throw` over the
45:  * discriminated-union return of `checkCapacity`.
46:  */
47: export class ConcurrentCapExceededError extends Error {
48: 	readonly idleSessions: readonly AgentChatSession[];
49: 	readonly cap: number;
50: 
51: 	constructor(payload: {
52: 		idleSessions: readonly AgentChatSession[];
53: 		cap: number;
54: 	}) {
55: 		super(
56: 			`Concurrent ACP session cap of ${payload.cap} reached. Cancel one of the ${payload.idleSessions.length} idle session(s) and try again.`
57: 		);
58: 		this.name = "ConcurrentCapExceededError";
59: 		this.idleSessions = payload.idleSessions;
60: 		this.cap = payload.cap;
61: 	}
62: }
63: 
64: // ============================================================================
65: // Panel abstraction
66: // ============================================================================
67: 
68: /**
69:  * Subset of `vscode.WebviewPanel` used by the registry. Declaring a structural
70:  * subtype here keeps the registry testable without importing the full VS Code
71:  * API surface into unit tests.
72:  *
73:  * `AgentChatPanel` (T026) will satisfy this shape.
74:  */
75: export interface AgentChatPanelLike {
76: 	readonly viewType: string;
77: 	reveal(...args: unknown[]): void;
78: 	dispose(): void;
79: 	onDidDispose(cb: () => void): { dispose: () => void };
80: }
81: 
82: // ============================================================================
83: // Registry
84: // ============================================================================
85: 
86: export class AgentChatRegistry {
87: 	private readonly sessionsById = new Map<string, AgentChatSession>();
88: 	private readonly panelsBySessionId = new Map<string, AgentChatPanelLike>();
89: 	private readonly runnersBySessionId = new Map<
90: 		string,
91: 		AgentChatRunnerHandle
92: 	>();
93: 
94: 	private readonly _onDidChange = new EventEmitter<void>();
95: 	readonly onDidChange = this._onDidChange.event;
96: 
97: 	/** Register a session. Overwrites any existing entry for the same id. */
98: 	registerSession(session: AgentChatSession): void {
99: 		this.sessionsById.set(session.id, session);
100: 		this._onDidChange.fire();
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	/** Update an existing session's lifecycle state or other fields in place. */
104: 	updateSession(sessionId: string, patch: Partial<AgentChatSession>): void {
105: 		const existing = this.sessionsById.get(sessionId);
106: 		if (!existing) {
107: 			return;
108: 		}
109: 		Object.assign(existing, patch);
110: 		this._onDidChange.fire();
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	/** Remove a session and dispose any attached panel or runner. */
114: 	removeSession(sessionId: string): void {
115: 		const panel = this.panelsBySessionId.get(sessionId);
116: 		if (panel) {
117: 			panel.dispose();
118: 			this.panelsBySessionId.delete(sessionId);
119: 		}
120: 		const runner = this.runnersBySessionId.get(sessionId);
121: 		if (runner) {
122: 			runner.dispose();
123: 			this.runnersBySessionId.delete(sessionId);
124: 		}
125: 		this.sessionsById.delete(sessionId);
126: 		this._onDidChange.fire();
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	getSession(sessionId: string): AgentChatSession | undefined {
130: 		return this.sessionsById.get(sessionId);
131: 	}
132: 
133: 	listActive(): AgentChatSession[] {
134: 		const out: AgentChatSession[] = [];
135: 		for (const session of this.sessionsById.values()) {
136: 			if (!TERMINAL_STATES.has(session.lifecycleState)) {
137: 				out.push(session);
138: 			}
139: 		}
140: 		return out;
141: 	}
142: 
143: 	listRecent(): AgentChatSession[] {
144: 		const out: AgentChatSession[] = [];
145: 		for (const session of this.sessionsById.values()) {
146: 			if (TERMINAL_STATES.has(session.lifecycleState)) {
147: 				out.push(session);
148: 			}
149: 		}
150: 		return out;
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
154: 	// Panels (FR-008: one panel per session)
155: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
156: 
157: 	/**
158: 	 * Attach a panel to a session. Throws if the session already has a panel
159: 	 * (the caller should use {@link focusPanel} or {@link openPanelFor} instead).
160: 	 */
161: 	attachPanel(sessionId: string, panel: AgentChatPanelLike): void {
162: 		if (this.panelsBySessionId.has(sessionId)) {
163: 			throw new Error(
164: 				`[agent-chat-registry] session ${sessionId} already has a panel — focus the existing panel instead of attaching a new one`
165: 			);
166: 		}
167: 		this.panelsBySessionId.set(sessionId, panel);
168: 		panel.onDidDispose(() => {
169: 			this.panelsBySessionId.delete(sessionId);
170: 			this._onDidChange.fire();
171: 		});
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	getPanel(sessionId: string): AgentChatPanelLike | undefined {
175: 		return this.panelsBySessionId.get(sessionId);
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	/** Focus an existing panel if present; returns whether a panel was focused. */
179: 	focusPanel(sessionId: string): boolean {
180: 		const panel = this.panelsBySessionId.get(sessionId);
181: 		if (!panel) {
182: 			return false;
183: 		}
184: 		panel.reveal();
185: 		return true;
186: 	}
187: 
188: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
189: 	// Runners
190: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
191: 
192: 	/** Attach a runner to a session. Throws if one is already attached. */
193: 	attachRunner(sessionId: string, runner: AgentChatRunnerHandle): void {
194: 		if (this.runnersBySessionId.has(sessionId)) {
195: 			throw new Error(
196: 				`[agent-chat-registry] session ${sessionId} already has a runner`
197: 			);
198: 		}
199: 		this.runnersBySessionId.set(sessionId, runner);
200: 	}
201: 
202: 	getRunner(sessionId: string): AgentChatRunnerHandle | undefined {
203: 		return this.runnersBySessionId.get(sessionId);
204: 	}
205: 
206: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
207: 	// T073 — concurrent-cap enforcement
208: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
209: 
210: 	/**
211: 	 * Decide whether a new session of the given `source` may be started given
212: 	 * `cap`. Only ACP sessions count against the cap — cloud sessions run on
213: 	 * a provider and never spawn a local subprocess (FR-003, research R5).
214: 	 *
215: 	 * When the cap would be exceeded, the returned `idleSessions` list is:
216: 	 *   - filtered to non-terminal ACP sessions (candidates that could be
217: 	 *     cancelled to free a slot),
218: 	 *   - sorted so sessions currently in `waiting-for-input` appear first
219: 	 *     (they are the best cancellation candidates), and within each bucket
220: 	 *     sorted by `updatedAt` ascending (longest-idle first).
221: 	 *
222: 	 * The caller (T073a) uses this to build the QuickPick UX.
223: 	 */
224: 	checkCapacity(
225: 		source: AgentChatSession["source"],
226: 		cap: number
227: 	): CapacityCheck {
228: 		if (source !== "acp") {
229: 			return { ok: true };
230: 		}
231: 		const liveAcp: AgentChatSession[] = [];
232: 		for (const session of this.sessionsById.values()) {
233: 			if (
234: 				session.source === "acp" &&
235: 				!TERMINAL_STATES.has(session.lifecycleState)
236: 			) {
237: 				liveAcp.push(session);
238: 			}
239: 		}
240: 		if (liveAcp.length < cap) {
241: 			return { ok: true };
242: 		}
243: 		const idleSessions = liveAcp.slice().sort(idleFirstThenOldest);
244: 		return { ok: false, idleSessions, cap };
245: 	}
246: 
247: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
248: 	// Shutdown flush
249: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
250: 
251: 	/**
252: 	 * Stamp non-terminal ACP sessions as `ended-by-shutdown`, hand the snapshot
253: 	 * to `flushSink` (exactly once), then dispose every attached runner.
254: 	 *
255: 	 * The sink is responsible for persisting the snapshot (typically
256: 	 * `AgentChatSessionStore.flushForDeactivation()` wrapping it). The registry
257: 	 * does not persist directly — it only coordinates in-memory state.
258: 	 */
259: 	async shutdown(
260: 		flushSink: (sessions: AgentChatSession[]) => Promise<void>
261: 	): Promise<void> {
262: 		const snapshot: AgentChatSession[] = [];
263: 		for (const session of this.sessionsById.values()) {
264: 			if (
265: 				session.source === "acp" &&
266: 				!TERMINAL_STATES.has(session.lifecycleState)
267: 			) {
268: 				session.lifecycleState = "ended-by-shutdown";
269: 				session.endedAt = session.endedAt ?? Date.now();
270: 				session.updatedAt = Date.now();
271: 			}
272: 			snapshot.push(session);
273: 		}
274: 
275: 		await flushSink(snapshot);
276: 
277: 		for (const runner of this.runnersBySessionId.values()) {
278: 			try {
279: 				runner.dispose();
280: 			} catch {
281: 				// Best-effort — a failing dispose on one runner should not block
282: 				// the others.
283: 			}
284: 		}
285: 		this.runnersBySessionId.clear();
286: 		this._onDidChange.fire();
287: 	}
288: 
289: 	dispose(): void {
290: 		for (const runner of this.runnersBySessionId.values()) {
291: 			try {
292: 				runner.dispose();
293: 			} catch {
294: 				// Intentionally ignored.
295: 			}
296: 		}
297: 		for (const panel of this.panelsBySessionId.values()) {
298: 			try {
299: 				panel.dispose();
300: 			} catch {
301: 				// Intentionally ignored.
302: 			}
303: 		}
304: 		this.runnersBySessionId.clear();
305: 		this.panelsBySessionId.clear();
306: 		this.sessionsById.clear();
307: 		this._onDidChange.dispose();
308: 	}
309: }
310: 
311: function idleFirstThenOldest(a: AgentChatSession, b: AgentChatSession): number {
312: 	const aIdle = a.lifecycleState === "waiting-for-input";
313: 	const bIdle = b.lifecycleState === "waiting-for-input";
314: 	if (aIdle !== bIdle) {
315: 		return aIdle ? -1 : 1;
316: 	}
317: 	return a.updatedAt - b.updatedAt;
318: }

(End of file - total 318 lines)
</content>

/**
 * AgentChatRegistry — in-memory index of live chat sessions, panels, and
 * runners for the Agent Chat Panel feature.
 *
 * Responsibilities:
 *   - Track one `WebviewPanel` per session (FR-008 invariant).
 *   - Track the active runner (`AcpChatRunner` or `CloudChatAdapter`) for each
 *     session via the forward-compatible `AgentChatRunnerHandle` interface.
 *   - Partition sessions into active vs. recent buckets for the tree view.
 *   - Drive the shutdown flush handed to `AgentChatSessionStore`.
 *
 * Persistence is out of scope for this module — the store owns disk state.
 * The registry is the authority on "what is live in this process right now".
 *
 * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/contracts/agent-chat-panel-protocol.md §2
 * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/data-model.md §5 (Invariants)
 */

import { EventEmitter } from "vscode";

1: /**
2:  * AgentChatSessionStore — workspaceState-backed persistence for the Agent
3:  * Chat Panel feature.
4:  *
5:  * Implements:
6:  *   - T009a: manifest + transcript CRUD, concurrency mutex,
7:  *            onDidChangeManifest emitter, schema-versioned layout.
8:  *   - T009b: archival (count / size thresholds), retention eviction with
9:  *            orphaned-worktree migration, flushForDeactivation()
10:  *            single-atomic-update contract, idempotent restart restore.
11:  *
12:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/contracts/agent-chat-session-storage.md
13:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/data-model.md §3
14:  */
15: 
16: import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
17: import { existsSync } from "node:fs";
18: import { EventEmitter } from "vscode";
19: import { AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS, logTelemetry } from "./telemetry";
20: import {
21: 	AGENT_CHAT_STORAGE_KEYS,
22: 	type AgentChatSession,
23: 	type AgentChatSettings,
24: 	type ChatMessage,
25: 	type CreateSessionInput,
26: 	DEFAULT_AGENT_CHAT_SETTINGS,
27: 	type OrphanedWorktreeEntry,
28: 	type OrphanedWorktreeList,
29: 	type SessionManifest,
30: 	type SessionManifestEntry,
31: 	type SystemChatMessage,
32: 	TERMINAL_STATES,
33: 	transcriptKeyFor,
34: } from "./types";
35: 
36: // ============================================================================
37: // Dependencies injected by constructor
38: // ============================================================================
39: 
40: /**
41:  * Minimal Memento interface (compatible with `vscode.Memento` /
42:  * `ExtensionContext.workspaceState`).
43:  */
44: export interface AgentChatMemento {
45: 	get<T>(key: string, defaultValue?: T): T | undefined;
46: 	update(key: string, value: unknown): Thenable<void>;
47: 	keys?(): readonly string[];
48: }
49: 
50: /**
51:  * Archive IO abstraction. Injected so tests do not touch the filesystem.
52:  * Production callers pass an implementation backed by
53:  * `vscode.workspace.fs.writeFile` rooted at `globalStorageUri`.
54:  */
55: export interface AgentChatArchiveWriter {
56: 	/**
57: 	 * Append archived messages to a session's archive folder. Returns the
58: 	 * filename (relative to `<globalStorage>/agent-chat/<sessionId>/`) that
59: 	 * received the append.
60: 	 */
61: 	appendLines(sessionId: string, messages: ChatMessage[]): Promise<string>;
62: 
63: 	/** Read a page of archived messages from disk. */
64: 	readLines(
65: 		sessionId: string,
66: 		offset: number,
67: 		limit: number
68: 	): Promise<ChatMessage[]>;
69: }
70: 
71: export interface AgentChatSessionStoreOptions {
72: 	workspaceState: AgentChatMemento;
73: 	archive: AgentChatArchiveWriter;
74: 	/**
75: 	 * Called during `initialize()` for every non-terminal cloud session so
76: 	 * spec 016's polling can re-attach. Optional — omit in tests that do not
77: 	 * exercise cloud sessions.
78: 	 */
79: 	cloudAttach?: (cloudSessionLocalId: string) => Promise<void>;
80: 	/**
81: 	 * Synchronous predicate used during `initialize()` to determine whether a
82: 	 * worktree path still exists. Defaults to `fs.existsSync`; overridden in
83: 	 * tests.
84: 	 */
85: 	worktreeExists?: (absolutePath: string) => boolean;
86: 	/** Clock injection for deterministic tests. Defaults to `Date.now()`. */
87: 	now?: () => number;
88: }
89: 
90: // ============================================================================
91: // Internal constants
92: // ============================================================================
93: 
94: /** Archive threshold (research R4). */
95: const MAX_TRANSCRIPT_MESSAGES = 10_000;
96: const MAX_TRANSCRIPT_BYTES = 2 * 1024 * 1024; // 2 MB
97: const ARCHIVE_PIVOT_RATIO = 0.25;
98: 
99: /** Retention cap (research R4). */
100: const MAX_MANIFEST_SESSIONS = 100;
101: 
102: /** Current schema version. */
103: const SCHEMA_VERSION = 1;
104: 
105: // ============================================================================
106: // Persisted transcript shape
107: // ============================================================================
108: 
109: interface TranscriptFile {
110: 	schemaVersion: typeof SCHEMA_VERSION;
111: 	sessionId: string;
112: 	messages: ChatMessage[];
113: 	hasArchive: boolean;
114: 	latestArchiveFile?: string;
115: 	updatedAt: number;
116: }
117: 
118: // ============================================================================
119: // Store
120: // ============================================================================
121: 
122: export class AgentChatSessionStore {
123: 	private readonly workspaceState: AgentChatMemento;
124: 	private readonly archive: AgentChatArchiveWriter;
125: 	private readonly cloudAttach?: (localId: string) => Promise<void>;
126: 	private readonly worktreeExists: (absolutePath: string) => boolean;
127: 	private readonly now: () => number;
128: 
129: 	/**
130: 	 * Per-session aggregate cache. Rebuilt in memory on every write so the
131: 	 * canonical state lives in `workspaceState`; this cache is just for O(1)
132: 	 * reads between writes.
133: 	 */
134: 	private readonly sessions = new Map<string, AgentChatSession>();
135: 
136: 	/**
137: 	 * Serialisation chain. Every mutation `.enqueue()`s onto this chain so
138: 	 * concurrent writes never interleave.
139: 	 */
140: 	private writeChain: Promise<void> = Promise.resolve();
141: 
142: 	private readonly _onDidChangeManifest = new EventEmitter<SessionManifest>();
143: 	readonly onDidChangeManifest = this._onDidChangeManifest.event;
144: 
145: 	constructor(options: AgentChatSessionStoreOptions) {
146: 		this.workspaceState = options.workspaceState;
147: 		this.archive = options.archive;
148: 		this.cloudAttach = options.cloudAttach;
149: 		this.worktreeExists = options.worktreeExists ?? existsSync;
150: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
154: 	// Public API
155: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
156: 
157: 	/**
158: 	 * Load manifest, run restart-restore logic (FR-019a/b/c), and populate the
159: 	 * in-memory cache. Safe to call multiple times; subsequent calls are no-ops
160: 	 * for already-restored sessions (idempotent).
161: 	 *
162: 	 * @see contracts/agent-chat-session-storage.md §6.1
163: 	 */
164: 	async initialize(): Promise<void> {
165: 		const manifest = this.readManifest();
166: 		for (const entry of manifest.sessions) {
167: 			this.sessions.set(entry.id, this.hydrateSession(entry));
168: 		}
169: 
170: 		let manifestDirty = false;
171: 		for (const entry of manifest.sessions) {
172: 			const wasStamped = await this.restoreEntry(entry);
173: 			manifestDirty = manifestDirty || wasStamped;
174: 		}
175: 
176: 		if (manifestDirty) {
177: 			await this.persistManifest(manifest);
178: 		}
179: 	}
180: 
181: 	/**
182: 	 * Per-entry restart-restore logic. Returns `true` when the entry was mutated
183: 	 * (caller persists the manifest once at the end).
184: 	 */
185: 	private async restoreEntry(entry: SessionManifestEntry): Promise<boolean> {
186: 		let mutated = false;
187: 		const previousState = entry.lifecycleState;
188: 
189: 		if (this.shouldStampAcpShutdown(entry)) {
190: 			entry.lifecycleState = "ended-by-shutdown";
191: 			entry.endedAt = entry.endedAt ?? this.now();
192: 			entry.updatedAt = this.now();
193: 			this.refreshCacheEntry(entry);
194: 			mutated = true;
195: 		}
196: 
197: 		if (
198: 			entry.source === "acp" &&
199: 			(previousState !== "ended-by-shutdown" ||
200: 				!this.hasTailShutdownMarker(entry.id)) &&
201: 			this.needsShutdownMarker(entry.id)
202: 		) {
203: 			await this.appendSystemMessageInternal(entry.id, {
204: 				kind: "ended-by-shutdown",
205: 				content: "Session ended because VS Code closed.",
206: 			});
207: 		}
208: 
209: 		if (
210: 			entry.source === "cloud" &&
211: 			!TERMINAL_STATES.has(entry.lifecycleState) &&
212: 			entry.cloudSessionLocalId &&
213: 			this.cloudAttach
214: 		) {
215: 			await this.cloudAttach(entry.cloudSessionLocalId);
216: 		}
217: 
218: 		if (
219: 			entry.executionTargetKind === "worktree" &&
220: 			entry.worktreePath &&
221: 			!this.worktreeExists(entry.worktreePath) &&
222: 			this.needsWorktreeCleanedMarker(entry.id)
223: 		) {
224: 			await this.appendSystemMessageInternal(entry.id, {
225: 				kind: "worktree-cleaned",
226: 				content: `Worktree at ${entry.worktreePath} no longer exists on disk.`,
227: 			});
228: 		}
229: 
230: 		return mutated;
231: 	}
232: 
233: 	private shouldStampAcpShutdown(entry: SessionManifestEntry): boolean {
234: 		return entry.source === "acp" && !TERMINAL_STATES.has(entry.lifecycleState);
235: 	}
236: 
237: 	/**
238: 	 * Create a new session and persist the manifest + empty transcript.
239: 	 */
240: 	createSession(input: CreateSessionInput): Promise<AgentChatSession> {
241: 		return this.enqueue(async () => {
242: 			const manifest = this.readManifest();
243: 			const id = randomUUID();
244: 			const createdAt = input.createdAt ?? this.now();
245: 
246: 			const session: AgentChatSession = {
247: 				id,
248: 				source: input.source,
249: 				agentId: input.agentId,
250: 				agentDisplayName: input.agentDisplayName,
251: 				capabilities: input.capabilities,
252: 				selectedModeId: input.selectedModeId,
253: 				selectedModelId: input.selectedModelId,
254: 				availableModels: input.availableModels,
255: 				currentModelId: input.currentModelId,
256: 				executionTarget: input.executionTarget,
257: 				lifecycleState: "initializing",
258: 				trigger: input.trigger,
259: 				worktree: input.worktree,
260: 				cloud: input.cloud,

parts.text:
**Considering new provider options**

I’m thinking about registering a new provider and what commands to use. I might need some actions, like refreshing or opening a panel. A webview could help, with options for a refresh button or card buttons that open a session or a cloud agent panel. It seems like I should also look into existing command options for the cloud agent panel. There’s a bit to explore here!

I’m checking the existing cloud-agent commands and webview command patterns before wiring the orchestration interactions, so the new surface can delegate to current commands instead of owning new behavior.

1: /**
2:  * Cloud Agent Commands
3:  *
4:  * VS Code commands for provider selection, credential configuration,
5:  * session dispatch, cancel, and external link actions.
6:  *
7:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
8:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/plan.md
9:  */
10: 
11: import { Uri, commands, window, workspace } from "vscode";
12: import { dirname } from "node:path";
13: import type { AgentSessionStorage } from "../features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage";
14: import type { AgentPollingService } from "../features/cloud-agents/agent-polling-service";
15: import type { CloudAgentProvider } from "../features/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-provider";
16: import type { ProviderRegistry } from "../features/cloud-agents/provider-registry";
17: import { logInfo, logError } from "../features/cloud-agents/logging";
18: import {
19: 	ProviderError,
20: 	SessionStatus,
21: 	type AgentSession,
22: 	type SpecTask,
23: 	type SessionContext,
24: } from "../features/cloud-agents/types";
25: import {
26: 	getCurrentBranch,
27: 	getRemoteUrl,
28: } from "../features/devin/git-validator";
29: 
30: // ============================================================================
31: // Types
32: // ============================================================================
33: 
34: /**
35:  * Shape of the tree item passed from the Spec Explorer when
36:  * the "Run on Cloud" inline action is clicked on a task-item or task-group.
37:  */
38: export interface DispatchTreeItem {
39: 	readonly contextValue?: string;
40: 	readonly task?: {
41: 		readonly id: string;
42: 		readonly title: string;
43: 		readonly priority?: string;
44: 	};
45: 	readonly specName?: string;
46: 	readonly filePath?: string;
47: 	readonly parentName?: string;
48: }
49: 
50: // ============================================================================
51: // Command IDs
52: // ============================================================================
53: 
54: export const CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS = {
55: 	SELECT_PROVIDER: "gatomia.selectProvider",
56: 	CHANGE_PROVIDER: "gatomia.changeProvider",
57: 	CONFIGURE_PROVIDER: "gatomia.configureProvider",
58: 	DISPATCH_TASK: "gatomia.dispatchTask",
59: 	DISPATCH_FULL_SPEC: "gatomia.dispatchFullSpec",
60: 	CANCEL_SESSION: "gatomia.cancelSession",
61: 	REMOVE_SESSION: "gatomia.removeSession",
62: 	REFRESH: "gatomia.refreshCloudAgents",
63: } as const;
64: 
65: // ============================================================================
66: // Command Registration
67: // ============================================================================
68: 
69: /**
70:  * Options for Cloud Agent command registration.
71:  */
72: export interface CloudAgentCommandOptions {
73: 	registry: ProviderRegistry;
74: 	sessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage;
75: 	pollingService?: AgentPollingService;
76: 	onSessionCreated?: () => void;
77: 	onRefresh?: () => void;
78: }
79: 
80: /**
81:  * Registers all Cloud Agent commands with VS Code.
82:  * @param opts - Command dependencies
83:  * @returns Disposable array for cleanup
84:  *
85:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
86:  */
87: export function registerCloudAgentCommands(
88: 	opts: ProviderRegistry | CloudAgentCommandOptions
89: ): { dispose(): void }[] {
90: 	const options: CloudAgentCommandOptions =
91: 		"registry" in opts ? opts : { registry: opts };
92: 	const { registry } = options;
93: 	const disposables: { dispose(): void }[] = [];
94: 
95: 	disposables.push(
96: 		commands.registerCommand(
97: 			CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.SELECT_PROVIDER,
98: 			async (providerId?: string) => {
99: 				await handleSelectProvider(options, providerId);
100: 			}
101: 		)
102: 	);
103: 
104: 	disposables.push(
105: 		commands.registerCommand(CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.CHANGE_PROVIDER, async () => {
106: 			await handleChangeProvider(options);
107: 		})
108: 	);
109: 
110: 	disposables.push(
111: 		commands.registerCommand(
112: 			CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.CONFIGURE_PROVIDER,
113: 			async (providerId?: string) => {
114: 				await handleConfigureProvider(registry, providerId);
115: 			}
116: 		)
117: 	);
118: 
119: 	disposables.push(
120: 		commands.registerCommand(
121: 			CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.DISPATCH_TASK,
122: 			async (itemOrTaskId?: DispatchTreeItem | string, specPath?: string) => {
123: 				await handleDispatchTask(options, itemOrTaskId, specPath);
124: 			}
125: 		)
126: 	);
127: 
128: 	disposables.push(
129: 		commands.registerCommand(
130: 			CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.CANCEL_SESSION,
131: 			async (arg?: string | { session?: { localId?: string } }) => {
132: 				const localId = typeof arg === "string" ? arg : arg?.session?.localId;
133: 				await handleCancelSession(options, localId);
134: 			}
135: 		)
136: 	);
137: 
138: 	disposables.push(
139: 		commands.registerCommand(
140: 			CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.REMOVE_SESSION,
141: 			async (arg?: string | { session?: { localId?: string } }) => {
142: 				const localId = typeof arg === "string" ? arg : arg?.session?.localId;
143: 				await handleRemoveSession(options, localId);
144: 			}
145: 		)
146: 	);
147: 
148: 	disposables.push(
149: 		commands.registerCommand(CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.REFRESH, async () => {
150: 			await handleRefresh(options);
151: 		})
152: 	);
153: 
154: 	disposables.push(
155: 		commands.registerCommand(
156: 			CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.DISPATCH_FULL_SPEC,
157: 			async (item?: DispatchTreeItem) => {
158: 				await handleDispatchFullSpec(options, item);
159: 			}
160: 		)
161: 	);
162: 
163: 	return disposables;
164: }
165: 
166: // ============================================================================
167: // Command Handlers
168: // ============================================================================
169: 
170: async function handleSelectProvider(
171: 	opts: CloudAgentCommandOptions,
172: 	providerId?: string
173: ): Promise<void> {
174: 	const { registry } = opts;
175: 	try {
176: 		let selectedId = providerId;
177: 
178: 		if (!selectedId) {
179: 			const providers = registry.getAll();
180: 			if (providers.length === 0) {
181: 				window.showInformationMessage(
182: 					"No cloud agent providers are available."
183: 				);
184: 				return;
185: 			}
186: 
187: 			const items = providers.map((p) => ({
188: 				label: p.metadata.displayName,
189: 				description: p.metadata.description,
190: 				id: p.metadata.id,
191: 			}));
192: 
193: 			const picked = await window.showQuickPick(items, {
194: 				placeHolder: "Select a cloud agent provider",
195: 			});
196: 
197: 			if (!picked) {
198: 				return;
199: 			}
200: 			selectedId = picked.id;
201: 		}

**Evaluating orchestration implementation**

I'm looking to implement substantial edits, likely using apply_patch. I might inspect the package.json commands around the cloud agent menu and consider adding an orchestration view. A new provider class, service, and UI page/components could be necessary, along with possible tests and documentation updates. I also need to think about command IDs for opening sessions and refreshing. After reviewing, it seems we need the new view as a webview in the package.json, specifically in the gatomia container. I'll ensure the provider is registered correctly in the extension.

**Considering orchestration bootstrap and UI**

I think orchestration needs to bootstrap after the agent chat, using resources if available. I should inspect the cloud session UI since it seems there isn't a page, and it might not be utilized. The task mentions reusing components from cloud-agent-progress-view for orchestration, which could be possible even if the page is missing. I also need to create documentation with YAML front matter and add tests for various states. I’ll check test setup path patterns and the package.json for view contributions as well.

**Planning session command handling**

I need to create a command to open a session, possibly through the webview by using postMessage to the provider to execute existing commands. This approach would apply the same for opening a cloud external session. It's essential to inspect any provider patterns for webview message handling, like using view.webview.onDidReceiveMessage and commands.executeCommand. The task suggests reusing current event emitters and telemetry helpers, so I might log through outputChannel.appendLine and consider existing telemetry methods for efficiency.

**Analyzing active and recent tasks**

I’m evaluating how to store active and recent tasks, possibly using `store.listActive/recent` and `cloudSessionStorage.getAll`. I want to classify them into columns like Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed. For example, I could mark states like initializing and running as active, while waiting-for-input would go under waiting. I need to ensure I include lifecycle metadata and maintain current tree behavior while making this data accessible in a web-friendly format. It might be too invasive, though.

**Clarifying tree-view behavior**

The task states that I should "preserve current tree-view behavior" while allowing normalized data to flow to a webview-friendly bridge, creating a single source of truth. This likely means a read model service should support both the running agents tree provider and the orchestration provider, at least for agent chat. I might keep current Running and Cloud Agents views, but I could also have a service produce an orchestration snapshot without altering the existing trees. I’m thinking about refining data grouping for the running agents provider and possibly adding an onDidChange event service too.

**Evaluating event emitters for updates**

Since the cloud registry doesn’t have an event emitter and session storage lacks events, I need to refresh the orchestration provider using `pollingService.onSessionUpdated`. The provider registry may need to change only upon commands with an `onRefresh` callback. It seems like `bootstrapCloudAgents` has a method for refreshing the UI. I’m considering providing a general refresh event emitter for tracking provider changes, session creations, and polling updates. The orchestration provider could subscribe to various changes, like `agentChat.store.onDidChangeManifest` and a custom cloud refresh event from `bootstrapCloudAgents`.

**Exploring UI commands**

I’m considering that maybe the command IDs aren’t registered, but the default `<viewId>.focus` exists, which might be safer. For webview and tree view, there are likely focus commands that I could call and just ignore any failures. The provider might implement these. I want the UI to be simple yet visually compelling, maybe using cards with columns, counts, and status badges. I’ll also think about including docs with YAML front matter, Obsidian style, and possibly creating a directory.

**Organizing file structure and tests**

I need to ensure all current file names are in kebab-case, and every new file should follow that too. I'll use apply_patch for any edits or additions. It's possible to add multiple files in one patch. I think I should inspect the package command menus to see if I can add an orchestration view. Also, for the webview view when there are no sessions, a welcome screen might not be necessary, as providers can handle empty states. I'll also check the tests folder for existing unit tests and consider where to add any new ones.

**Considering read model service**

I need to think about creating a read model service and using it only in the provider. For the current tree behavior, I might consider using helper functions from the running-agents provider. I’m pondering if it makes sense to export icons, but I’m leaning towards no. I should also inspect any existing wiki documentation conventions in the front matter and take a look at the docs and architecture available.
I’m checking the existing docs front-matter conventions and any existing UI styles before I write the new orchestration files, so the added docs and webview feel native to the repo.

parts.text:
**Planning architecture changes**

I need to edit files using apply_patch for manual edits only. Reviewing documentation is essential, specifically sample front matter. I shouldn’t use "Todo" and need to implement things fully. I’m considering creating the orchestration service and UI features, along with updating package.json. There’s no docs folder right now, which is necessary. Testing is crucial too! I’m thinking about cloud agent bootstrapping and instance sharing, looking for ways to streamline orchestration between components.

**Evaluating bootstrap integration**

I'm considering registering in `bootstrapAgentChat` if cloud globals are set, as the cloud bootstrap runs before agent chat. I need reactive updates without manual reload from session-store, registry, or cloud-provider changes. I could utilize events like `store.onDidChangeManifest`, but `ProviderRegistry` lacks an event emitter. Adding a minimal `EventEmitter` for various commands could help. Updating `AgentSessionStorage` with an emitter would be useful too. I need to maintain current structure while exposing normalized data for easier integration. It’s essential to merge local and cloud aspects efficiently.

**Balancing tree-view behavior and data**

I want to maintain the current tree-view behavior while also providing normalized data to a bridge for a single source of truth. I could have `RunningAgentsTreeProvider` retain its current functionality while using a read model helper for agent sessions. However, the aim is to ensure the tree provider reflects the new orchestration tree while preserving existing behavior. Ultimately, I want to ensure that the prototype maintains consistency and clarity, making the data accessible and coherent.

**Considering webview integration**

I'm thinking about adding `gatomia.views.orchestration` as a webview in the gatomia container. First, I need to register the WebviewViewProvider. It seems I could use commands from this webview to open either a new or existing cloud experience, depending on what's available. If there's an existing agent chat, perhaps I could have a command to open that. There’s also a mention of navigating back to agent or cloud experiences. Another consideration is refreshing the session state and inspecting the current status to keep everything updated.

**Exploring orchestration commands**

I'm considering adding a new command, `gatomia.orchestration.refresh`, to refresh the orchestration provider, along with the runningAgents tree and the cloud tree. This would also call `pollingService.pollOnce(true)` if available. I think I might also need `gatomia.views.orchestration.focus`. For the UI, I need a registry page name and a layout with columns or cards for Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed states. Normalizing these state categories could help, so I’m thinking of defining `OrchestrationBucket` with statuses like active, waiting, completed, and failed.

**Considering cloud navigation**

I’m thinking about how, if a cloud session is opened in chat, the user can potentially navigate back to the agent-chat or cloud experience through commands associated with cloud items. It might not be strictly necessary at this moment. I wonder if I should refer to the documentation, possibly linking to resources like "Running-Agents-Prototype" and "Orchestration-UI-Contract," to provide clarity later on in the phases. Let's see if that helps!

**Considering toolbars for views**

I'm thinking about whether we need to include a toolbar in the view title. It seems like adding it could potentially improve the user experience, but I'm also wondering if it's really necessary. Maybe it's an optional feature? I could look into the package.json file to see how the title and menus are currently set up. It might help me decide if adding that toolbar is a good idea or if I should leave it out.

**Considering telemetry options**

I’m thinking I could just use the output channel and maybe implement commands for telemetry. It seems like it could be beneficial to reuse some "existing telemetry helpers." If I import `logTelemetry` from the agent-chat telemetry, that might streamline things. So, I'll need to check the file to confirm all of this. Let's see if that makes everything come together nicely!

I’m checking the current telemetry helper and the cloud-agent session link behavior before I finalize the provider contract, so the new view can log sensible actions and open the right destination for each session type.

1: /**
2:  * CloudChatAdapter — read-only bridge from spec 016 cloud-agent sessions to
3:  * the Agent Chat Panel runtime (T060).
4:  *
5:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/tasks.md T060
6:  *
7:  * Responsibilities:
8:  *   - Subscribe to {@link AgentPollingService.onSessionUpdated} events.
9:  *   - Map provider-canonical {@link SessionStatus} transitions into
10:  *     {@link AgentChatEvent} values consumed by `AgentChatPanel`.
11:  *   - Route cancels to the active provider's `cancelSession`.
12:  *   - Refuse follow-up input by not exposing `submit`/`retry` (FR-003).
13:  *   - Enforce one-adapter-per-cloudSessionLocalId via an internal registry so
14:  *     repeated `attach()` calls are idempotent and `dispose()` is reliably
15:  *     balanced.
16:  */
17: 
18: import type { Disposable } from "vscode";
19: import type {
20: 	AgentChatEvent,
21: 	AgentChatRunnerHandle,
22: 	SessionLifecycleState,
23: } from "./types";

27: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
28: //
29: // We intentionally keep the dependency on `src/features/cloud-agents/**`
30: // one-way by re-declaring the *subset* of interfaces the adapter needs rather
31: // than importing the concrete classes. This keeps the agent-chat bundle free
32: // of cloud-agents-specific imports in tests and avoids the transitive VS Code
33: // API surface for unit isolation.
34: 
35: export interface AgentSession {
36: 	readonly localId: string;
37: 	readonly providerId: string;
38: 	status:
39: 		| "pending"
40: 		| "running"
41: 		| "blocked"
42: 		| "completed"
43: 		| "failed"
44: 		| "cancelled";
45: 	readonly errorMessage?: string;
46: }
47: 
48: export interface AgentSessionUpdatedEvent {
49: 	readonly localId: string;
50: 	readonly session: AgentSession;
51: }
52: 
53: export interface AgentPollingLike {
54: 	onSessionUpdated(
55: 		listener: (event: AgentSessionUpdatedEvent) => void
56: 	): Disposable;
57: }
58: 
59: export interface CloudAgentProvider {
60: 	readonly metadata: { readonly id: string };
61: 	cancelSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void>;
62: }
63: 
64: export interface ProviderRegistry {
65: 	getActive(): CloudAgentProvider | undefined;
66: 	get(id: string): CloudAgentProvider | undefined;
67: }
68: 
69: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
70: // Adapter
71: // ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
72: 
73: export interface CloudChatAdapterAttachOptions {
74: 	/** `AgentSession.localId` (spec 016 storage key). */
75: 	cloudSessionLocalId: string;
76: 	/** `AgentChatSession.id` — the agent-chat-side identifier. */
77: 	sessionId: string;
78: 	poller: AgentPollingLike;
79: 	registry: ProviderRegistry;
80: 	onEvent: (event: AgentChatEvent) => void;
81: }
82: 
83: const STATUS_TO_LIFECYCLE: Record<
84: 	AgentSession["status"],
85: 	SessionLifecycleState
86: > = {
87: 	pending: "initializing",
88: 	running: "running",
89: 	blocked: "waiting-for-input",
90: 	completed: "completed",
91: 	failed: "failed",
92: 	cancelled: "cancelled",
93: };
94: 
95: export class CloudChatAdapter implements AgentChatRunnerHandle {
96: 	/**
97: 	 * One-adapter-per-cloudSessionLocalId to keep `attach()` idempotent.
98: 	 * Keyed by localId because that is the provider-authoritative identifier.
99: 	 */
100: 	private static readonly instances = new Map<string, CloudChatAdapter>();
101: 
102: 	readonly sessionId: string;
103: 	private readonly cloudSessionLocalId: string;
104: 	private readonly registry: ProviderRegistry;
105: 	private readonly onEvent: (event: AgentChatEvent) => void;
106: 	private readonly subscription: Disposable;
107: 	private lastStatus: AgentSession["status"] | undefined;
108: 	private disposed = false;
109: 
110: 	private constructor(options: CloudChatAdapterAttachOptions) {
111: 		this.sessionId = options.sessionId;
112: 		this.cloudSessionLocalId = options.cloudSessionLocalId;
113: 		this.registry = options.registry;
114: 		this.onEvent = options.onEvent;
115: 		this.subscription = options.poller.onSessionUpdated((event) => {
116: 			this.handleUpdate(event);
117: 		});
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	/**
121: 	 * Attach or re-attach to a cloud session. Safe to call repeatedly — the
122: 	 * first call creates the adapter; subsequent calls with the same
123: 	 * `cloudSessionLocalId` return the same instance.
124: 	 */
125: 	static attach(options: CloudChatAdapterAttachOptions): CloudChatAdapter {
126: 		const existing = CloudChatAdapter.instances.get(
127: 			options.cloudSessionLocalId
128: 		);
129: 		if (existing && !existing.disposed) {
130: 			return existing;
131: 		}
132: 		const adapter = new CloudChatAdapter(options);
133: 		CloudChatAdapter.instances.set(options.cloudSessionLocalId, adapter);
134: 		return adapter;
135: 	}
136: 
137: 	async cancel(): Promise<void> {
138: 		const provider = this.registry.getActive();
139: 		if (!provider) {
140: 			return;
141: 		}
142: 		await provider.cancelSession(this.cloudSessionLocalId);
143: 	}
144: 
145: 	dispose(): void {
146: 		if (this.disposed) {
147: 			return;
148: 		}
149: 		this.disposed = true;
150: 		this.subscription.dispose();
151: 		const current = CloudChatAdapter.instances.get(this.cloudSessionLocalId);
152: 		if (current === this) {
153: 			CloudChatAdapter.instances.delete(this.cloudSessionLocalId);
154: 		}
155: 	}
156: 
157: 	private handleUpdate(event: AgentSessionUpdatedEvent): void {
158: 		if (event.localId !== this.cloudSessionLocalId) {
159: 			return;
160: 		}
161: 		const next = event.session.status;
162: 		const at = Date.now();
163: 		if (next !== this.lastStatus) {
164: 			const from: SessionLifecycleState =
165: 				this.lastStatus === undefined
166: 					? "initializing"
167: 					: STATUS_TO_LIFECYCLE[this.lastStatus];
168: 			const to = STATUS_TO_LIFECYCLE[next];
169: 			this.lastStatus = next;
170: 			this.onEvent({
171: 				type: "lifecycle/transitioned",
172: 				sessionId: this.sessionId,
173: 				from,
174: 				to,
175: 				at,
176: 			});
177: 		}
178: 		if (next === "failed" && event.session.errorMessage) {
179: 			this.onEvent({
180: 				type: "error",
181: 				sessionId: this.sessionId,
182: 				category: "cloud-dispatch-failed",
183: 				message: event.session.errorMessage,
184: 				retryable: true,
185: 				at,
186: 			});
187: 		}
188: 	}
189: }

(End of file - total 189 lines)
</content>

If I run `npm test` on all affected areas, that might be thorough enough, especially if those targeted tests pass. I should also add to the package.json to include a view contribution and command button in the view title.

**Outlining file updates**

I’m considering outlining what files I need to add or update. I know I need to include `orchestration-read-model.ts` and `orchestration-view-provider.ts`. **Evaluating data source integration**

I'm considering how to share helper functions related to using a normalized data source. It seems like the RunningAgentsTreeProvider could utilize a helper function for descriptions. **Examining service and model integration**

I'm considering whether tests can proceed without the readModel being mandatory. It might work if I make it optional. 528: 			await opts.pollingService.pollOnce(true);
529: 		}
530: 		opts.onRefresh?.();
531: 		logInfo("Cloud Agent sessions refreshed");
532: 	} catch (error) {
533: 		logError("Failed to refresh cloud agent sessions", error);
534: 	}
535: }
536: 
537: async function handleCancelSession(
538: 	opts: CloudAgentCommandOptions,
539: 	localId?: string
540: ): Promise<void> {
541: 	const { registry, sessionStorage, onRefresh } = opts;
542: 	try {
543: 		if (!localId) {
544: 			return;
545: 		}
546: 		if (!sessionStorage) {
547: 			return;
548: 		}
549: 
550: 		const session = await sessionStorage.getById(localId);
551: 		if (!session) {
552: 			return;
553: 		}
554: 
555: 		if (session.isReadOnly) {
556: 			window.showWarningMessage(
557: 				"Cannot cancel a read-only session from a previous provider."
558: 			);
559: 			return;
560: 		}
561: 
562: 		const provider = registry.get(session.providerId);
563: 		if (provider && session.localId) {
564: 			await provider.cancelSession(session.localId);
565: 		}
566: 
567: 		await sessionStorage.update(localId, {
568: 			status: SessionStatus.CANCELLED,
569: 			completedAt: Date.now(),
570: 		});
571: 		onRefresh?.();
572: 		logInfo(`Session cancelled: ${localId}`);
573: 		window.showInformationMessage("Session cancelled.");
574: 	} catch (error) {
575: 		logError("Failed to cancel session", error);
576: 		window.showErrorMessage("Failed to cancel session.");
577: 	}
578: }
579: 
580: async function handleRemoveSession(
581: 	opts: CloudAgentCommandOptions,
582: 	localId?: string
583: ): Promise<void> {
584: 	try {
585: 		if (!localId) {
586: 			return;
587: 		}
588: 		if (!opts.sessionStorage) {
589: 			return;
590: 		}
591: 
592: 		await opts.sessionStorage.delete(localId);
593: 		opts.onRefresh?.();
594: 		logInfo(`Session removed: ${localId}`);
595: 	} catch (error) {
596: 		logError("Failed to remove session", error);
597: 	}
598: }

**Assessing development process**

I need to implement some significant changes, focusing on manual edits with the `apply_patch` function. I'm considering not pushing or committing changes since the user wants to complete phase 01 without any interruptions. The developer prefers not to push unless explicitly asked, which must take priority over repository guidelines. I'll need to create documentation for architecture and orchestration, and determine the file structure for minimal implementation while inspecting relevant commands in `package.json`. It's crucial I carefully register components in the extension.

**Planning global singletons**

I need to ensure that singletons are surfaced globally, particularly for the agent chat, and consider adding globals for the cloud agent registry, session storage, and polling service. I should avoid duplicating initialization. After confirming both globals exist, I could create the provider; alternatively, I might register it inside `bootstrapCloudAgents`. It seems simpler to set cloud globals in that bootstrap and later call `registerOrchestrationPrototype(context)` if everything exists. I have to remember to create the necessary files, services, and documentation as a part of this.

**Considering document organization**

I’m wondering if it's okay to include explicit links even if the page is missing. It could be better to create only the requested documents, but I’m also thinking about whether I should provide a `Running-Agents-Model` document as additional support. It's not requested, but perhaps having wiki links that remain unresolved wouldn’t hurt. I just want to make sure I’m being helpful and providing valuable resources without overdoing it!

**Considering button functionality**

I'm thinking about how to improve the user interface. Maybe having buttons like "Open in Agent Chat" for local access and "Open Cloud Agents" for cloud access could work. I wonder if there should be one button to open a session and another for accessing the source. For local, it would open a session to the agent chat, and for cloud, it might need to open a source panel, but I'm not sure since there isn't a command for that.

**Evaluating orchestration and storage events**

I'm thinking about how the orchestration provider's subscription to the registry and polling relates to sessionStorage create, delete, and update actions. It seems there isn’t an event emitter for storage changes during these commands, while polling updates have their own events. The manual dispatch, cancel, or remove calls onRefresh are something I still need to consider closely. It's a bit tricky to piece together, but I’m working through it!

**Considering directory structure and file management**

I’m thinking about adding new events and possibly using `logTelemetry("orchestration.view.opened"...`. That seems straightforward. But I need to verify existing documentation paths and folders before creating any new directories. I’m unsure if `apply_patch` can handle nested directories if they don’t exist. It might be safer to check with a bash command to see what’s there first. I’ll need to run `ls` commands for the relevant directories to confirm their existence.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md
+---
+name: running-agents-prototype
+description: Phase 01 orchestration prototype baseline for active and recent agent sessions
+author: OpenCode
+version: v0.0.1
+---
+
+# Running Agents Prototype
+
+See also: [[Running-Agents-Model]], [[Orchestration-UI-Contract]]
+
+## Goal
+
+Deliver the smallest viable orchestration surface by composing the existing Agent Chat and Cloud Agents runtime state into one read model and one webview.
+
+## Reused Modules
+
+- `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts` remains the tree-first summary for agent-chat sessions.
+- `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts` remains the canonical session-detail surface for local and bridged chat sessions.
+- `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts` remains the cloud tree surface.
+- `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store.ts` and `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry.ts` remain the source of truth for local session lifecycle.
+- `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage.ts`, `src/features/cloud-agents/provider-registry.ts`, and `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-polling-service.ts` remain the source of truth for cloud progress.
+- `ui/src/page-registry.tsx` and `src/utils/get-webview-content.ts` remain the shared page-registration and webview bootstrap path.
+
+## New Modules
+
+- `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` provides a normalized projection for local and cloud sessions.
+- `src/providers/orchestration-view-provider.ts` owns the orchestration webview binding and command bridge.
+- `ui/src/features/orchestration/index.tsx` renders the prototype dashboard.
+
+## Prototype Slice
+
+- One read model merges agent-chat sessions and cloud-agent sessions.
+- One webview presents four buckets: Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed.
+- Each card supports open session, refresh state, inspect lifecycle metadata, and jump back to the existing surface.
+- Existing trees stay intact and continue to update independently.
+
+## Constraints
+
+- No second orchestration state store.
+- No new routing layer.
+- No mutation path inside the read model beyond delegating to current commands.
+- Cloud sessions open externally when there is no richer in-extension detail surface yet.
*** Add File: docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md
+---
+name: orchestration-ui-contract
+description: UI contract for the phase 01 orchestration prototype
+author: OpenCode
+version: v0.0.1
+---
+
+# Orchestration UI Contract
+
+See also: [[Running-Agents-Prototype]]
+
+## Inbound Messages
+
+- `orchestration/ready`: webview asks the extension for the current snapshot.
+- `orchestration/refresh`: webview asks the extension to refresh cloud polling and republish the snapshot.
+- `orchestration/open-session`: webview asks the extension to open the selected session in the best existing surface.
+- `orchestration/open-existing-surface`: webview asks the extension to reveal the original surface for a session source.
+- `orchestration/open-external`: webview asks the extension to open an external provider URL.
+
+## Outbound Messages
+
+- `orchestration/snapshot`: extension publishes the normalized orchestration snapshot.
+
+## Snapshot Shape
+
+- `sessions[]`: normalized session cards with `id`, `source`, `sourceSessionId`, `title`, `agentName`, `state`, `bucket`, timestamps, blocking metadata, worktree metadata, and open-session routing hints.
+- `activeProvider`: current cloud provider metadata when present.
+- `generatedAt`: snapshot creation timestamp.
+- `degradedReasons[]`: user-facing degraded-state explanations.
+
+## Empty and Degraded States
+
+- Empty state explains that no local or cloud sessions exist yet.
+- Degraded state explains when cloud storage or provider wiring is unavailable.
+- Waiting sessions are surfaced as blocked work so they do not disappear inside the active list.
*** End Patch

+## Reused Modules
+
+- `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts` remains the tree-first summary for agent-chat sessions.
+- `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts` remains the canonical session-detail surface for local and bridged chat sessions.
+- `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts` remains the cloud tree surface.
+- `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store.ts` and `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry.ts` remain the source of truth for local session lifecycle.
+- `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage.ts`, `src/features/cloud-agents/provider-registry.ts`, and `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-polling-service.ts` remain the source of truth for cloud progress.
+- `ui/src/page-registry.tsx` and `src/utils/get-webview-content.ts` remain the shared page-registration and webview bootstrap path.
+
+## New Modules
+
+- `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` provides a normalized projection for local and cloud sessions.
+- `src/providers/orchestration-view-provider.ts` owns the orchestration webview binding and command bridge.
+- `ui/src/features/orchestration/index.tsx` renders the prototype dashboard.
+
+## Prototype Slice
+
+- One read model merges agent-chat sessions and cloud-agent sessions.
+- One webview presents four buckets: Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed.
+- Each card supports open session, refresh state, inspect lifecycle metadata, and jump back to the existing surface.
+- Existing trees stay intact and continue to update independently.
+
+## Constraints
+
+- No second orchestration state store.
+- No new routing layer.
+- No mutation path inside the read model beyond delegating to current commands.
+- Cloud sessions open externally when there is no richer in-extension detail surface yet.

+++ /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+name: orchestration-ui-contract
+description: UI contract for the phase 01 orchestration prototype
+author: OpenCode
+version: v0.0.1
+---
+
+# Orchestration UI Contract
+
+See also: [[Running-Agents-Prototype]]
+
+## Inbound Messages
+
+- `orchestration/ready`: webview asks the extension for the current snapshot.
+- `orchestration/refresh`: webview asks the extension to refresh cloud polling and republish the snapshot.
+- `orchestration/open-session`: webview asks the extension to open the selected session in the best existing surface.
+- `orchestration/open-existing-surface`: webview asks the extension to reveal the original surface for a session source.
+- `orchestration/open-external`: webview asks the extension to open an external provider URL.
+
+## Outbound Messages
+
+- `orchestration/snapshot`: extension publishes the normalized orchestration snapshot.
+
+## Snapshot Shape
+
+- `sessions[]`: normalized session cards with `id`, `source`, `sourceSessionId`, `title`, `agentName`, `state`, `bucket`, timestamps, blocking metadata, worktree metadata, and open-session routing hints.
+- `activeProvider`: current cloud provider metadata when present.
+- `generatedAt`: snapshot creation timestamp.
+- `degradedReasons[]`: user-facing degraded-state explanations.
+
+## Empty and Degraded States
+
+- Empty state explains that no local or cloud sessions exist yet.
+- Degraded state explains when cloud storage or provider wiring is unavailable.
+- Waiting sessions are surfaced as blocked work so they do not disappear inside the active list.

61: export interface OrchestrationReadModelOptions {
62: 	readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;

64: 	readonly cloudSessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage;
65: 	readonly cloudProviderRegistry?: ProviderRegistry;
66: 	readonly agentChatStoreChangeEvent: Event<unknown>;
67: 	readonly onCloudSessionsChanged?: (listener: () => void) => { dispose(): void };
68: 	readonly onCloudProviderChanged?: (listener: () => void) => { dispose(): void };
69: 	readonly now?: () => number;
70: }
71: 
72: export class OrchestrationReadModel {
73: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
74: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
75: 	private readonly cloudSessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage;
76: 	private readonly cloudProviderRegistry?: ProviderRegistry;
77: 	private readonly now: () => number;
78: 	private readonly subscriptions: Array<{ dispose(): void }> = [];
79: 	private readonly listeners = new Set<() => void>();
80: 
81: 	constructor(options: OrchestrationReadModelOptions) {
82: 		this.store = options.store;
83: 		this.registry = options.registry;
84: 		this.cloudSessionStorage = options.cloudSessionStorage;
85: 		this.cloudProviderRegistry = options.cloudProviderRegistry;
86: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
87: 
88: 		this.subscriptions.push(
89: 			options.agentChatStoreChangeEvent(() => {
90: 				this.emitChanged();
91: 			})
92: 		);
93: 		this.subscriptions.push(
94: 			this.registry.onDidChange(() => {
95: 				this.emitChanged();
96: 			})
97: 		);
98: 		if (options.onCloudSessionsChanged) {
99: 			this.subscriptions.push(
100: 				options.onCloudSessionsChanged(() => {
101: 					this.emitChanged();
102: 				})
103: 			);
104: 		}
105: 		if (options.onCloudProviderChanged) {
106: 			this.subscriptions.push(
107: 				options.onCloudProviderChanged(() => {
108: 					this.emitChanged();
109: 				})
110: 			);
111: 		}
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	onDidChange(listener: () => void): { dispose(): void } {
115: 		this.listeners.add(listener);
116: 		return {
117: 			dispose: () => {
118: 				this.listeners.delete(listener);
119: 			},
120: 		};
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	async snapshot(): Promise<OrchestrationSnapshot> {
124: 		const sessions: OrchestrationSessionProjection[] = [];
125: 		const degradedReasons: string[] = [];
126: 
127: 		const [agentChatSessions, cloudSessions] = await Promise.all([
128: 			this.collectAgentChatSessions(),
129: 			this.collectCloudSessions(degradedReasons),
130: 		]);
131: 
132: 		sessions.push(...agentChatSessions, ...cloudSessions);
133: 		sessions.sort(compareSessions);
134: 
135: 		const activeProvider = this.cloudProviderRegistry?.getActive();
136: 
137: 		return {
138: 			sessions,
139: 			activeProvider: activeProvider
140: 				? {
141: 						id: activeProvider.metadata.id,
142: 						displayName: activeProvider.metadata.displayName,
143: 				  }
144: 				: undefined,
145: 			generatedAt: this.now(),
146: 			degradedReasons,
147: 		};
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	dispose(): void {
151: 		for (const subscription of this.subscriptions) {
152: 			try {
153: 				subscription.dispose();
154: 			} catch {
155: 				// best-effort
156: 			}
157: 		}
158: 		this.subscriptions.length = 0;
159: 		this.listeners.clear();
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	private emitChanged(): void {
163: 		for (const listener of [...this.listeners]) {
164: 			try {
165: 				listener();
166: 			} catch {
167: 				// best-effort
168: 			}
169: 		}
170: 	}
171: 
172: 	private async collectAgentChatSessions(): Promise<
173: 		OrchestrationSessionProjection[]
174: 	> {
175: 		const [active, recent] = await Promise.all([
176: 			this.store.listActive(),
177: 			this.store.listRecent(),
178: 		]);
179: 		return [...active, ...recent].map((session) => toAgentChatProjection(session));
180: 	}
181: 
182: 	private async collectCloudSessions(
183: 		degradedReasons: string[]
184: 	): Promise<OrchestrationSessionProjection[]> {
185: 		if (!this.cloudSessionStorage) {
186: 			degradedReasons.push("Cloud agent session storage is unavailable.");
187: 			return [];
188: 		}
189: 		const sessions = await this.cloudSessionStorage.getAll();
190: 		return sessions.map((session) => toCloudProjection(session));
191: 	}
192: }
193: 
194: function toAgentChatProjection(
195: 	session: AgentChatSession
196: ): OrchestrationSessionProjection {
197: 	return {
198: 		id: `agent-chat:${session.id}`,

1: import {
2: 	commands,
3: 	env,
4: 	type CancellationToken,
5: 	type Disposable,
6: 	type ExtensionContext,
7: 	type WebviewView,
8: 	type WebviewViewProvider,
9: 	type WebviewViewResolveContext,
10: 	Uri,
11: } from "vscode";
12: import {
13: 	AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS,
14: 	logTelemetry,
15: } from "../features/agent-chat/telemetry";
16: import {
17: 	type OrchestrationReadModel,
18: 	type OrchestrationSessionProjection,
19: } from "../features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model";
20: import { getWebviewContent } from "../utils/get-webview-content";
21: 
22: interface OrchestrationViewProviderOptions {
23: 	readonly context: ExtensionContext;
24: 	readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
25: 	readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
26: }
27: 
28: export class OrchestrationViewProvider implements WebviewViewProvider {
29: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.views.orchestration";
30: 
31: 	private readonly context: ExtensionContext;
32: 	private readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
33: 	private readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
34: 	private readonly disposables: Disposable[] = [];
35: 	private view: WebviewView | undefined;
36: 
37: 	constructor(options: OrchestrationViewProviderOptions) {
38: 		this.context = options.context;
39: 		this.readModel = options.readModel;
40: 		this.outputChannel = options.outputChannel;
41: 
42: 		this.disposables.push(
43: 			this.readModel.onDidChange(() => {
44: 				this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
45: 					this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
46: 						`[Orchestration] snapshot push failed: ${formatError(error)}`
47: 					);
48: 				});
49: 			})
50: 		);
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	resolveWebviewView(
54: 		webviewView: WebviewView,
55: 		_context: WebviewViewResolveContext,
56: 		_token: CancellationToken
57: 	): void {
58: 		this.view = webviewView;
59: 
60: 		webviewView.webview.options = {
61: 			enableScripts: true,
62: 			localResourceRoots: [this.context.extensionUri],
63: 		};
64: 		webviewView.webview.html = getWebviewContent(
65: 			webviewView.webview,
66: 			this.context.extensionUri,
67: 			"orchestration"
68: 		);
69: 
70: 		this.disposables.push(
71: 			webviewView.webview.onDidReceiveMessage((message) => {
72: 				this.handleWebviewMessage(message).catch((error) => {
73: 					this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
74: 						`[Orchestration] message handling failed: ${formatError(error)}`
75: 					);
76: 				});
77: 			})
78: 		);
79: 
80: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
81: 			this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
82: 				`[Orchestration] initial snapshot failed: ${formatError(error)}`
83: 			);
84: 		});
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	async reveal(): Promise<void> {
88: 		await commands.executeCommand(`${OrchestrationViewProvider.viewType}.focus`);
89: 		this.view?.show?.(true);
90: 	}
91: 
92: 	refresh(): void {
93: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
94: 			this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
95: 				`[Orchestration] refresh failed: ${formatError(error)}`
96: 			);
97: 		});
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	dispose(): void {
101: 		for (const disposable of this.disposables) {
102: 			try {
103: 				disposable.dispose();
104: 			} catch {
105: 				// best-effort
106: 			}
107: 		}
108: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
109: 	}
110: 
111: 	private async handleWebviewMessage(raw: unknown): Promise<void> {
112: 		const message = raw as { type?: string; payload?: unknown };
113: 		if (!message?.type) {
114: 			return;
115: 		}
116: 
117: 		switch (message.type) {
118: 			case "orchestration/ready":
119: 				await this.pushSnapshot();
120: 				return;
121: 			case "orchestration/refresh":
122: 				await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents").catch(() => {
123: 					// best-effort
124: 				});
125: 				await this.pushSnapshot();
126: 				return;
127: 			case "orchestration/open-session": {
128: 				const sessionId = (message.payload as { sessionId?: string } | undefined)
129: 					?.sessionId;
130: 				if (!sessionId) {
131: 					return;
132: 				}
133: 				const snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
134: 				const session = snapshot.sessions.find((entry) => entry.id === sessionId);
135: 				if (session) {
136: 					await this.openSession(session);
137: 				}
138: 				return;
139: 			}
140: 			case "orchestration/open-existing-surface": {
141: 				const source = (message.payload as { source?: string } | undefined)?.source;
142: 				if (source === "cloud-agent") {
143: 					await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
144: 					return;
145: 				}
146: 				await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.agentChat.openPanel");
147: 				return;
148: 			}
149: 			case "orchestration/open-external": {
150: 				const url = (message.payload as { url?: string } | undefined)?.url;
151: 				if (url) {
152: 					await env.openExternal(Uri.parse(url));
153: 				}
154: 				return;
155: 			}
156: 			default:
157: 				return;
158: 		}
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	private async pushSnapshot(): Promise<void> {
162: 		if (!this.view) {
163: 			return;
164: 		}
165: 		const snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
166: 		await this.view.webview.postMessage({
167: 			type: "orchestration/snapshot",
168: 			payload: snapshot,
169: 		});
170: 	}
171: 
172: 	private async openSession(
173: 		session: OrchestrationSessionProjection
174: 	): Promise<void> {
175: 		logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.PANEL_OPENED, {
176: 			sessionId: session.sourceSessionId,
177: 			source: session.source,
178: 			surface: "orchestration",
179: 		});
180: 
181: 		if (session.openSessionCommand.kind === "agent-chat") {
182: 			await commands.executeCommand(
183: 				"gatomia.agentChat.openForSession",
184: 				session.openSessionCommand.sessionId
185: 			);
186: 			return;
187: 		}
188: 
189: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
190: 			await env.openExternal(Uri.parse(session.externalUrl));
191: 			return;
192: 		}
193: 
194: 		await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
195: 	}
196: }
197: 
198: function formatError(error: unknown): string {
199: 	return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
200: }

(End of file - total 200 lines)
</content>
